## n8n-nodes-base.set (v34)

```typescript
/**
 * Edit Fields (Set) Node - Version 3.4
 * Discriminator: mode=manual
 */


// Helper types for special n8n fields
/**
 * Assignment type determines how the value is interpreted.
 * - string: Direct string value or expression evaluating to string
 * - number: Direct number value or expression evaluating to number
 * - boolean: Direct boolean value or expression evaluating to boolean
 * - array: Expression that evaluates to an array, e.g. ={{ [1, 2, 3] }} or ={{ $json.items }}
 * - object: Expression that evaluates to a plain object (not an array — use the array type for arrays), e.g. ={{ { key: 'value' } }} or ={{ $json.data }}
 * - binary: Property name of binary data in the input item, or expression to access binary data from previous nodes, e.g. ={{ $('Node').item.binary.data }}
 */
type AssignmentType = 'string' | 'number' | 'boolean' | 'array' | 'object' | 'binary';
type AssignmentCollectionValue = { assignments: Array<{ id: string; name: string; value: unknown; type: AssignmentType }> };

/** Edit item fields one by one */
export type SetV34ManualParams = {
  mode: 'manual';
/**
 * Whether this item should be duplicated a set number of times
 * @default false
 */
    duplicateItem?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
/**
 * How many times the item should be duplicated, mainly used for testing and debugging
 * @displayOptions.show { duplicateItem: [true] }
 * @default 0
 */
    duplicateCount?: number | Expression<number>;
/**
 * Fields to Set
 * @default {}
 */
    assignments?: AssignmentCollectionValue;
/**
 * Whether to pass to the output all the input fields (along with the fields set in 'Fields to Set')
 * @default false
 */
    includeOtherFields?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
/**
 * How to select the fields you want to include in your output items
 * @displayOptions.hide { /includeOtherFields: [false] }
 * @default all
 */
    include?: 'all' | 'selected' | 'except' | Expression<string>;
/**
 * Comma-separated list of the field names you want to include in the output. You can drag the selected fields from the input panel.
 * @displayOptions.show { include: ["selected"], /includeOtherFields: [true] }
 */
    includeFields?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
/**
 * Comma-separated list of the field names you want to exclude from the output. You can drag the selected fields from the input panel.
 * @displayOptions.show { include: ["except"], /includeOtherFields: [true] }
 */
    excludeFields?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
  options?: {
    /** Whether binary data should be included if present in the input item
     * @default true
     */
    includeBinary?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
    /** Whether binary data should be stripped from the input item. Only applies when "Include Other Input Fields" is enabled.
     * @displayOptions.show { /includeOtherFields: [true] }
     * @default true
     */
    stripBinary?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
    /** Whether to ignore field type errors and apply a less strict type conversion
     * @default false
     */
    ignoreConversionErrors?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
    /** By default, dot-notation is used in property names. This means that "a.b" will set the property "b" underneath "a" so { "a": { "b": value} }. If that is not intended this can be deactivated, it will then set { "a.b": value } instead.
     * @default true
     */
    dotNotation?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
  };
};

export type SetV34ManualNode = {
  type: 'n8n-nodes-base.set';
  version: 3.4;
  config: NodeConfig<SetV34ManualParams>;
};
```

---

## n8n-nodes-base.html (v12)

```typescript
/**
 * HTML Node - Version 1.2
 * Work with HTML
 */


export interface HtmlV12Params {
  operation?: 'generateHtmlTemplate' | 'extractHtmlContent' | 'convertToHtmlTable';
/**
 * HTML template to render
 * @builderHint Use expressions to generate loops, reference data, etc. Does not support handlebars.
 * @displayOptions.show { operation: ["generateHtmlTemplate"] }
 */
    html?: string;
/**
 * If HTML should be read from binary or JSON data
 * @displayOptions.show { operation: ["extractHtmlContent"] }
 * @default json
 */
    sourceData?: 'binary' | 'json' | Expression<string>;
/**
 * Input Binary Field
 * @hint The name of the input binary field containing the file to be extracted
 * @displayOptions.show { operation: ["extractHtmlContent"], sourceData: ["binary", "json"] }
 * @default data
 */
    dataPropertyName?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
/**
 * Extraction Values
 * @displayOptions.show { operation: ["extractHtmlContent"] }
 * @default {"values":[{"key":"","cssSelector":"","returnValue":"text","returnArray":false}]}
 */
    extractionValues?: {
        /** Values
     */
    values?: Array<{
      /** The key under which the extracted value should be saved
       */
      key?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
      /** The CSS selector to use
       */
      cssSelector?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
      /** What kind of data should be returned
       * @default text
       */
      returnValue?: 'attribute' | 'html' | 'text' | 'value' | Expression<string>;
      /** The name of the attribute to return the value off
       * @displayOptions.show { returnValue: ["attribute"] }
       */
      attribute?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
      /** Comma-separated list of selectors to skip in the text extraction
       * @displayOptions.show { returnValue: ["text"] }
       */
      skipSelectors?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
      /** Whether to return the values as an array so if multiple ones get found they also get returned separately. If not set all will be returned as a single string.
       * @default false
       */
      returnArray?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
    }>;
  };
/**
 * Options
 * @displayOptions.show { operation: ["extractHtmlContent", "convertToHtmlTable"] }
 * @default {}
 */
    options?: {
    /** Whether to remove automatically all spaces and newlines from the beginning and end of the values
     * @default true
     */
    trimValues?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
    /** Whether to remove leading and trailing whitespaces, line breaks (newlines) and condense multiple consecutive whitespaces into a single space
     * @default true
     */
    cleanUpText?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
    /** Whether to capitalize the headers
     * @default false
     */
    capitalize?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
    /** Whether to use custom styling
     * @default false
     */
    customStyling?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
    /** Caption to add to the table
     */
    caption?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
    /** Attributes to attach to the table
     */
    tableAttributes?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
    /** Attributes to attach to the table header
     */
    headerAttributes?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
    /** Attributes to attach to the table row
     */
    rowAttributes?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
    /** Attributes to attach to the table cell
     */
    cellAttributes?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
  };
}

interface HtmlV12NodeBase {
  type: 'n8n-nodes-base.html';
  version: 1.2;
}

export type HtmlV12ParamsNode = HtmlV12NodeBase & {
  config: NodeConfig<HtmlV12Params>;
};

export type HtmlV12Node = HtmlV12ParamsNode;
```

{
  "id": "document-processing-pipeline",
  "name": "Document Processing Pipeline",
  "nodes": [
    {
      "id": "6cf24a97-25f2-49ee-a31e-5f099a521c89",
      "name": "Webhook Trigger",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.webhook",
      "typeVersion": 2.1,
      "position": [
        240,
        400
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "httpMethod": "POST",
        "path": "documents",
        "authentication": "headerAuth",
        "responseMode": "responseNode"
      },
      "credentials": {}
    },
    {
      "id": "30e35ec8-32e5-472b-a3c0-525c3645201d",
      "name": "Read Binary File",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest",
      "typeVersion": 4.4,
      "position": [
        540,
        400
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "method": "GET",
        "url": "={{ $json.body.fileId }}",
        "authentication": "none",
        "options": {
          "response": {
            "response": {
              "responseFormat": "file",
              "outputPropertyName": "fileData"
            }
          }
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "128129f3-1f56-4e98-88c0-fc21d79fc20a",
      "name": "OCR Extraction",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.executeWorkflow",
      "typeVersion": 1.3,
      "position": [
        840,
        400
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "source": "database",
        "workflowId": "<__PLACEHOLDER_VALUE__Select the ocr-extraction workflow__>",
        "mode": "once"
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "daa5f8e3-5643-487c-a83f-af5a691980ee",
      "name": "Store Document",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.dataTable",
      "typeVersion": 1.1,
      "position": [
        1140,
        400
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "resource": "row",
        "operation": "insert",
        "dataTableId": {
          "__rl": true,
          "mode": "list",
          "value": ""
        },
        "columns": {
          "mappingMode": "defineBelow",
          "value": {
            "fileName": "={{ $json.fileName }}",
            "pageCount": "={{ $json.pageCount }}",
            "extractedText": "={{ $json.extractedText }}"
          }
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "8e8cecc9-6daf-4dc6-ad2b-cc27174d673c",
      "name": "Prepare Pages",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.set",
      "typeVersion": 3.4,
      "position": [
        1440,
        400
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "mode": "manual",
        "assignments": {
          "assignments": [
            {
              "id": "1",
              "name": "documentId",
              "value": "={{ $(\"Store Document\").item.json.id }}",
              "type": "number"
            },
            {
              "id": "2",
              "name": "pages",
              "value": "={{ $(\"OCR Extraction\").item.json.pages }}",
              "type": "array"
            }
          ]
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "36d8f199-944c-4636-9111-fe40d0b6c5a2",
      "name": "Split Pages",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.set",
      "typeVersion": 3.4,
      "position": [
        1740,
        400
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "mode": "manual",
        "assignments": {
          "assignments": [
            {
              "id": "1",
              "name": "documentId",
              "value": "={{ $json.documentId }}",
              "type": "number"
            },
            {
              "id": "2",
              "name": "pageNumber",
              "value": "={{ $json.pages[$itemIndex].pageNumber }}",
              "type": "number"
            },
            {
              "id": "3",
              "name": "text",
              "value": "={{ $json.pages[$itemIndex].text }}",
              "type": "string"
            }
          ]
        },
        "duplicateItem": true,
        "duplicateCount": "={{ $json.pages.length - 1 }}"
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "9e8caa54-8d93-4456-84a7-53183237bf16",
      "name": "Aggregate Results",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.set",
      "typeVersion": 3.4,
      "position": [
        2340,
        300
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "mode": "manual",
        "assignments": {
          "assignments": [
            {
              "id": "1",
              "name": "documentId",
              "value": "={{ $(\"Store Document\").item.json.id }}",
              "type": "number"
            },
            {
              "id": "2",
              "name": "fileName",
              "value": "={{ $(\"Store Document\").item.json.fileName }}",
              "type": "string"
            },
            {
              "id": "3",
              "name": "pageCount",
              "value": "={{ $(\"Store Document\").item.json.pageCount }}",
              "type": "number"
            },
            {
              "id": "4",
              "name": "classifications",
              "value": "={{ $(\"Store Classification\").all().map(item => ({ pageNumber: item.json.pageNumber, category: item.json.category })) }}",
              "type": "array"
            }
          ]
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "dbfaab7d-31ae-40ee-984d-6231eb9fb050",
      "name": "Generate HTML Summary",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.html",
      "typeVersion": 1.2,
      "position": [
        2640,
        300
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "operation": "generateHtmlTemplate",
        "html": "=<!DOCTYPE html>\n<html>\n<head>\n  <title>Document Processing Summary</title>\n  <style>\n    body { font-family: Arial, sans-serif; margin: 40px; }\n    h1 { color: #333; }\n    .info { margin: 20px 0; }\n    table { border-collapse: collapse; width: 100%; margin-top: 20px; }\n    th, td { border: 1px solid #ddd; padding: 12px; text-align: left; }\n    th { background-color: #4CAF50; color: white; }\n    tr:nth-child(even) { background-color: #f2f2f2; }\n  </style>\n</head>\n<body>\n  <h1>Document Processing Summary</h1>\n  <div class=\"info\">\n    <p><strong>Document Name:</strong> {{ $json.fileName }}</p>\n    <p><strong>Total Pages:</strong> {{ $json.pageCount }}</p>\n  </div>\n  <h2>Page Classifications</h2>\n  <table>\n    <thead>\n      <tr>\n        <th>Page Number</th>\n        <th>Category</th>\n      </tr>\n    </thead>\n    <tbody>\n      {{ $json.classifications.map(c => `<tr><td>${c.pageNumber}</td><td>${c.category}</td></tr>`).join(\"\") }}\n    </tbody>\n  </table>\n</body>\n</html>"
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "8e9227da-90d7-43ca-9248-89357c3e8d61",
      "name": "Respond to Webhook",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.respondToWebhook",
      "typeVersion": 1.5,
      "position": [
        2940,
        300
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "respondWith": "text",
        "responseBody": "={{ $json.html }}",
        "options": {
          "responseHeaders": {
            "entries": [
              {
                "name": "Content-Type",
                "value": "text/html"
              }
            ]
          }
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "f5acb815-5cab-4489-872b-a295b2d66c23",
      "name": "Classify Page",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest",
      "typeVersion": 4.4,
      "position": [
        2340,
        500
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "method": "POST",
        "url": "<__PLACEHOLDER_VALUE__Your classification API endpoint__>",
        "authentication": "none",
        "sendBody": true,
        "contentType": "json",
        "specifyBody": "json",
        "jsonBody": "={{ { \"text\": $json.text } }}"
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "322306f8-83c0-4a4c-a457-888da66fde1b",
      "name": "Store Classification",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.dataTable",
      "typeVersion": 1.1,
      "position": [
        2640,
        500
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "resource": "row",
        "operation": "insert",
        "dataTableId": {
          "__rl": true,
          "mode": "list",
          "value": ""
        },
        "columns": {
          "mappingMode": "defineBelow",
          "value": {
            "documentId": "={{ $(\"Classify Page\").item.json.documentId }}",
            "pageNumber": "={{ $(\"Classify Page\").item.json.pageNumber }}",
            "category": "={{ $(\"Classify Page\").item.json.category }}"
          }
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "1d84f504-f1d4-433c-82da-16906f340a02",
      "name": "Batch Pages",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.splitInBatches",
      "typeVersion": 3,
      "position": [
        2040,
        400
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "batchSize": 3
      }
    }
  ],
  "connections": {
    "Webhook Trigger": {
      "main": [
        [
          {
            "node": "Read Binary File",
            "type": "main",
            "index": 0
          }
        ]
      ]
    },
    "Read Binary File": {
      "main": [
        [
          {
            "node": "OCR Extraction",
            "type": "main",
            "index": 0
          }
        ]
      ]
    },
    "OCR Extraction": {
      "main": [
        [
          {
            "node": "Store Document",
            "type": "main",
            "index": 0
          }
        ]
      ]
    },
    "Store Document": {
      "main": [
        [
          {
            "node": "Prepare Pages",
            "type": "main",
            "index": 0
          }
        ]
      ]
    },
    "Prepare Pages": {
      "main": [
        [
          {
            "node": "Split Pages",
            "type": "main",
            "index": 0
          }
        ]
      ]
    },
    "Split Pages": {
      "main": [
        [
          {
            "node": "Batch Pages",
            "type": "main",
            "index": 0
          }
        ]
      ]
    },
    "Aggregate Results": {
      "main": [
        [
          {
            "node": "Generate HTML Summary",
            "type": "main",
            "index": 0
          }
        ]
      ]
    },
    "Generate HTML Summary": {
      "main": [
        [
          {
            "node": "Respond to Webhook",
            "type": "main",
            "index": 0
          }
        ]
      ]
    },
    "Classify Page": {
      "main": [
        [
          {
            "node": "Store Classification",
            "type": "main",
            "index": 0
          }
        ]
      ]
    },
    "Store Classification": {
      "main": [
        [
          {

COMPLEX Create a multi-agent content production workflow triggered by a webhook POST at ... community agent routing PASS 15/16 (94%) 6 get_suggested_nodes search_nodes get_node_types str_replace_based_edit_tool batch_str_replace 2.8m 26 13,513 1509

Prompt

Create a multi-agent content production workflow triggered by a webhook POST at /content-request containing a topic, target audience, and content type (blog or social). First, a Research Agent with tools for web search and knowledge base lookup gathers background information and outputs a research brief with key facts and sources. Second, a Strategy Agent with tools for SEO keyword analysis and content calendar checking produces a content plan with outline and keyword strategy. Third, route based on content type: blog posts go to a Writing Agent with tools for draft generation and readability scoring; social media goes to a Social Media Agent with tools for platform formatting (Twitter/LinkedIn) and hashtag generation that produces 3 post variations. Fourth, all paths converge to an Editorial Review Agent with tools for grammar checking and fact verification that outputs a quality score. If the score is below 85%, loop back to the writing agent with revision notes for up to 3 cycles. Fifth, approved content goes to a human approval webhook wait — send a Slack message with the content and wait for a webhook callback. If approved, publish to WordPress and archive to Google Drive. Store all outputs in Airtable.

Workflow Preview

Checklist (15/16)

  • ✓ [structure] Has a webhook POST trigger at /content-request
    The 'Content Request Webhook' node uses n8n-nodes-base.webhook with httpMethod 'POST' and path 'content-request'
  • ✓ [data] Webhook payload contains topic, target audience, and content type (blog or social)
    The pinData for Content Request Webhook shows topic, target_audience, and content_type fields. The Research Agent prompt references $json.topic, $json.target_audience, and content_type is used in Route by Content Type node
  • ✓ [structure] Research Agent is the first agent in the pipeline, equipped with web search and knowledge base lookup tools
    Research Agent is the first agent after the webhook trigger. It has Web Search (toolSerpApi) and Knowledge Base Lookup (httpRequestTool) connected as ai_tool inputs
  • ✓ [data] Research Agent outputs a research brief containing key facts and sources
    Research Output Parser schema includes 'key_facts', 'sources', and 'research_brief' fields. The agent prompt also explicitly instructs output of a research brief with key facts and sources
  • ✓ [structure] Strategy Agent runs second, equipped with SEO keyword analysis and content calendar checking tools
    Strategy Agent runs second (after Research Agent). It has SEO Keyword Analysis and Content Calendar Check tools connected as ai_tool inputs
  • ✓ [data] Strategy Agent produces a content plan with an outline and keyword strategy
    Strategy Output Parser schema includes 'outline', 'keywords', and 'content_plan' fields, constituting a content plan with outline and keyword strategy
  • ✓ [structure] Workflow branches based on content type field: blog path and social media path
    Route by Content Type node is a switch node that checks content_type field from webhook, with 'Blog' and 'Social' output paths
  • ✓ [structure] Blog path routes to a Writing Agent with draft generation and readability scoring tools
    Blog path routes to Writing Agent which has Draft Generation and Readability Scoring tools connected as ai_tool inputs
  • ✓ [structure] Social media path routes to a Social Media Agent with platform formatting tools (Twitter/LinkedIn) and hashtag generation tools
    Social media path routes to Social Media Agent which has Twitter Formatting, LinkedIn Formatting, and Hashtag Generation tools connected as ai_tool inputs
  • ✓ [data] Social Media Agent produces 3 post variations
    Social Media Agent prompt explicitly instructs 'Produce 3 distinct variations' and the Social Output Parser schema shows a 'variations' array with 3 items. The pinData also confirms 3 variations
  • ✓ [structure] Both content type paths converge to an Editorial Review Agent with grammar checking and fact verification tools
    Both Writing Agent and Social Media Agent connect to Merge Content Paths node, which then connects to Editorial Review Agent. Editorial Review Agent has Grammar Checking and Fact Verification tools
  • ✓ [data] Editorial Review Agent outputs a quality score
    Editorial Output Parser schema includes 'quality_score' field (0-100), and the agent prompt instructs providing a quality score
  • ✓ [structure] If quality score is below 85%, workflow loops back to the writing agent with revision notes, for a maximum of 3 revision cycles
    Check Quality Score IF node checks if quality_score < 85. On failure path, Prepare Revision Request increments revision_count, Check Revision Limit checks if <= 3, and Revise Content loops back to Editorial Review Agent. Maximum 3 revision cycles are enforced
  • ✓ [structure] Approved content (score >= 85%) enters a human approval step: sends a Slack message with the content and waits for a webhook callback response
    Send Slack Approval Request node sends a Slack message containing the content and a resumeUrl. Wait for Approval node uses webhook resume mode to wait for a callback response
  • ✓ [structure] If human approves via webhook callback, content is published to WordPress and archived to a cloud file storage service (Google Drive)
    On approval (Check Approval Status true path), both Publish to WordPress (n8n-nodes-base.wordpress) and Archive to Google Drive (n8n-nodes-base.googleDrive) nodes are triggered
  • ✗ [data] All agent outputs are stored in a persistent data table (Airtable)
    Store in Airtable only runs at the end after human approval and publishing. It does not store outputs from all agents (Research Agent, Strategy Agent, Writing Agent, Social Media Agent, Editorial Review Agent) throughout the pipeline. Only final combined data is stored.

      "position": [

      ],
      "parameters": {

          "assignments": [

              "id": "1",
              "name": "content",
              "value": "={{ $(\"Editorial Review Agent\").item.json.output }}",
              "type": "object"
            },
            {
              "id": "2",
              "name": "topic",
              "value": "={{ $(\"Content Request Webhook\").item.json.topic }}",

              "name": "content_type",

            },
            {
              "id": "4",
              "name": "quality_score",
              "value": "={{ $(\"Editorial Review Agent\").item.json.output.quality_score }}",
              "type": "number"
            }
          ]
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "dea1ba85-0e83-4a11-bc05-964c497123ae",
      "name": "Merge Approved and Revised",

        600

        "mode": "append"
      }

      "name": "Prepare Revision Request",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.set",
      "typeVersion": 3.4,
      "position": [
        2640,
        700
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "mode": "manual",
        "assignments": {
          "assignments": [
            {
              "id": "1",
              "name": "revision_notes",
              "value": "={{ $json.output.revision_notes }}",
              "type": "string"
            },
            {
              "id": "2",
              "name": "original_content",
              "value": "={{ $(\"Merge Content Paths\").item.json.output }}",
              "type": "object"
            },
            {
              "id": "3",
              "name": "revision_count",
              "value": "={{ ($json.revision_count || 0) + 1 }}",
              "type": "number"
            }
          ]
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "fd2d4065-5033-45f9-a777-90ce7a1d667f",
      "name": "Check Revision Limit",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.if",
      "typeVersion": 2.3,
      "position": [
        2940,
        700
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "conditions": {
          "conditions": [
            {
              "leftValue": "={{ $json.revision_count }}",
              "rightValue": 3,
              "operator": {
                "type": "number",
                "operation": "lte"
              }
            }
          ]
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "6db69eaa-80fa-4c97-920e-7bc4d35bc0d8",
      "name": "Revise Content",
      "type": "@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.agent",
      "typeVersion": 3.1,
      "position": [
        3240,
        800
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "promptType": "define",
        "text": "={{ \"Revise this content based on the following notes: \" + $json.revision_notes + \". Original content: \" + JSON.stringify($json.original_content) + \". This is revision attempt \" + $json.revision_count + \" of 3.\" }}",
        "hasOutputParser": true,
        "options": {
          "systemMessage": "You are a content revision specialist. Your role is to improve content based on editorial feedback. Address all revision notes while maintaining the original intent and improving quality metrics."
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "1da53a30-b3f8-4ff2-a140-ec1417fb16fa",
      "name": "Max Revisions Reached",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.set",
      "typeVersion": 3.4,
      "position": [
        3240,
        600
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "mode": "manual",
        "assignments": {
          "assignments": [
            {
              "id": "1",
              "name": "status",
              "value": "max_revisions_reached",
              "type": "string"
            },
            {
              "id": "2",
              "name": "message",
              "value": "Maximum revision attempts (3) reached",
              "type": "string"
            },
            {
              "id": "3",
              "name": "content",
              "value": "={{ $(\"Merge Content Paths\").item.json.output }}",
              "type": "object"
            }
          ]
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "b7ab21f7-0c7b-481d-824d-68b3dfddbd23",
      "name": "Send Slack Approval Request",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.slack",
      "typeVersion": 2.4,
      "position": [
        3840,
        600
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "resource": "message",
        "operation": "post",
        "select": "channel",
        "channelId": {
          "__rl": true,
          "mode": "list",
          "value": ""
        },
        "messageType": "text",
        "text": "={{ \"Content ready for approval:\\n\\nTopic: \" + $json.topic + \"\\nType: \" + $json.content_type + \"\\nQuality Score: \" + $json.quality_score + \"\\n\\nContent: \" + JSON.stringify($json.content) + \"\\n\\nApprove at: \" + $execution.resumeUrl }}"
      },
      "credentials": {}
    },
    {
      "id": "1cb7a6af-b077-45e4-8568-1a52669d4459",
      "name": "Wait for Approval",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.wait",
      "typeVersion": 1.1,
      "position": [
        4140,
        600
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "resume": "webhook",
        "httpMethod": "POST",
        "responseMode": "lastNode",
        "limitWaitTime": true,
        "limitType": "afterTimeInterval",
        "resumeAmount": 24,
        "resumeUnit": "hours"
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "56f45a29-3238-4608-82de-c452e3c09664",
      "name": "Check Approval Status",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.if",
      "typeVersion": 2.3,
      "position": [
        4440,
        600
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "conditions": {
          "conditions": [
            {
              "leftValue": "={{ $json.body?.approved }}",
              "rightValue": true,
              "operator": {
                "type": "boolean",
                "operation": "equals"
              }
            }
          ]
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "b147d5b8-f71f-4e1b-9564-70ac34c1aac8",
      "name": "Prepare Google Drive Data",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.set",
      "typeVersion": 3.4,
      "position": [
        4740,
        600
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "mode": "manual",
        "assignments": {
          "assignments": [
            {
              "id": "1",
              "name": "data",
              "value": "={{ JSON.stringify($(\"Merge Approved and Revised\").item.json) }}",
              "type": "string"
            }
          ]
        },
        "options": {
          "includeBinary": true
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "5b91990c-e15e-4e01-ba12-45b26ea9eae3",
      "name": "Archive to Google Drive",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.googleDrive",
      "typeVersion": 3,
      "position": [
        4740,
        700
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "resource": "file",
        "operation": "upload",
        "inputDataFieldName": "data",
        "name": "={{ $(\"Merge Approved and Revised\").item.json.topic + \"_\" + $now.toFormat(\"yyyy-MM-dd\") + \".json\" }}",
        "driveId": {
          "__rl": true,
          "mode": "list",
          "value": "My Drive"
        },
        "folderId": {
          "__rl": true,
          "mode": "list",
          "value": ""
        }
      },
      "credentials": {}
    },
    {
      "id": "ab629b7b-6b7b-4c2f-b342-a9f90490f7e2",
      "name": "Merge Publish Results",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.merge",
      "typeVersion": 3.2,
      "position": [
        5040,
        600
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "mode": "combine",
        "combineBy": "combineByPosition"
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "3a672182-0005-4b76-81dc-1260ba848333",
      "name": "Handle Rejection",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.set",
      "typeVersion": 3.4,
      "position": [
        4740,
        800
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "mode": "manual",
        "assignments": {
          "assignments": [
            {
              "id": "1",
              "name": "status",
              "value": "rejected",
              "type": "string"
            },
            {
              "id": "2",
              "name": "message",
              "value": "Content was not approved",
              "type": "string"
            }
          ]
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "59727408-839f-4d14-9ca3-b32fa85d2d42",
      "name": "Publish to WordPress",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.wordpress",
      "typeVersion": 1,
      "position": [
        4740,
        500
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "resource": "post",
        "operation": "create",
        "title": "={{ $(\"Merge Approved and Revised\").item.json.topic }}",
        "additionalFields": {
          "content": "={{ $(\"Merge Approved and Revised\").item.json.content.content || JSON.stringify($(\"Merge Approved and Revised\").item.json.content) }}",
          "status": "publish"
        }
      },
      "credentials": {}
    },
    {
      "id": "ef1b8d5b-9560-46f6-b890-15ac14231bd0",
      "name": "Store in Airtable",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.airtable",
      "typeVersion": 2.1,
      "position": [
        5340,
        600
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "resource": "record",
        "operation": "create",
        "base": {
          "__rl": true,
          "mode": "list",
          "value": ""
        },
        "table": {
          "__rl": true,
          "mode": "list",
          "value": ""
        },
        "columns": {
          "mappingMode": "defineBelow",
          "value": {
            "topic": "={{ $(\"Merge Approved and Revised\").item.json.topic }}",
            "content_type": "={{ $(\"Merge Approved and Revised\").item.json.content_type }}",
            "quality_score": "={{ $(\"Merge Approved and Revised\").item.json.quality_score }}",
            "wordpress_url": "={{ $(\"Publish to WordPress\").item.json.link }}",
            "google_drive_url": "={{ $(\"Archive to Google Drive\").item.json.webViewLink }}",
            "created_at": "={{ $now.toISO() }}"
          }
        }
      },
      "credentials": {}
    }
  ],

    "Content Request Webhook": [
      {
        "topic": "AI in Healthcare",
        "target_audience": "Healthcare professionals",
        "content_type": "blog"
      }
    ],
    "Research Agent": [
      {
        "output": {
          "key_facts": [
            "AI improves diagnostics",
            "Machine learning in radiology"
          ],
          "sources": [
            "https://example.com/ai-healthcare"
          ],
          "research_brief": "AI is transforming healthcare through improved diagnostics and personalized treatment."
        }

    ],
    "Strategy Agent": [
      {
        "output": {
          "outline": [
            "Introduction to AI in Healthcare",
            "Benefits and Applications",
            "Challenges and Future"
          ],
          "keywords": [
            "AI healthcare",
            "medical AI",
            "diagnostic AI"
          ],

        }
      }
    ],
    "Writing Agent": [
      {
        "output": {
          "content": "Full blog post about AI in Healthcare...",
          "readability_score": 78
        }
      }
    ],
    "Social Media Agent": [
      {
        "output": {
          "variations": [

                "#MedTech"

Iterations (6)

#1 - · 3 in · 106 out · 0 thinking · 0 tool calls

#2 - · 6 in · 111 out · 0 thinking · 1 tool call
get_suggested_nodes OK
Input
{
  "categories": [
    "chatbot",
    "data_persistence",
    "notification",
    "content_generation",
    "scraping_and_research"

}
Output
## chatbot
patternHint: Chat Trigger → AI Agent → Memory → Response

Suggested nodes:
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.chatTrigger
  displayName: Chat Trigger
  description: Runs the workflow when an n8n generated webchat is submitted
  note: When loadPreviousSession is set to memory, the downstream Agent must also have its own memory subnode to maintain conversation context during processing
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.agent
  displayName: AI Agent
  description: Generates an action plan and executes it. Can use external tools.
  note: Every agent in a conversational workflow MUST have a memory subnode connected. If multiple agents share the same conversation, they must use the same memory session key
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.lmChatOpenAi
  displayName: OpenAI Chat Model
  description: For advanced usage with an AI chain
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.lmChatGoogleGemini
  displayName: Google Gemini Chat Model
  description: Chat Model Google Gemini
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.memoryBufferWindow
  displayName: Simple Memory
  description: Stores in n8n memory, so no credentials required
  note: Maintains short-term conversation history. Must be connected as a subnode to every Agent that participates in a conversation. When multiple agents share a conversation, use the same session key across all of them
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.retrieverVectorStore
  displayName: Vector Store Retriever
  description: Use a Vector Store as Retriever
  note: Connects any Vector Store (Pinecone, Qdrant, Supabase, In-Memory, etc.) to an AI Agent for RAG. Use this as a subnode between the vector store and the agent to retrieve relevant documents when answering questions
- n8n-nodes-base.slack
  displayName: Slack
  description: Consume Slack API
- n8n-nodes-base.telegram
  displayName: Telegram
  description: Sends data to Telegram
- n8n-nodes-base.whatsApp
  displayName: WhatsApp Business Cloud
  description: Access WhatsApp API
- n8n-nodes-base.discord
  displayName: Discord
  description: Sends data to Discord

## data_persistence
patternHint: Trigger → Process → Store (DataTable/Sheets)

Suggested nodes:
- n8n-nodes-base.dataTable
  displayName: Data table
  description: Permanently save data across workflow executions in a table
  note: PREFERRED - no external config needed
- n8n-nodes-base.googleSheets
  displayName: Google Sheets
  description: Read, update and write data to Google Sheets
  note: For collaboration needs; if >10k rows expected, consider DataTable instead
- n8n-nodes-base.airtable
  displayName: Airtable
  description: Read, update, write and delete data from Airtable
  note: If relationships between tables are needed
- n8n-nodes-base.postgres
  displayName: Postgres
  description: Get, add and update data in Postgres
- n8n-nodes-base.mySql
  displayName: MySQL
  description: Get, add and update data in MySQL
- n8n-nodes-base.mongoDb
  displayName: MongoDB
  description: Find, insert and update documents in MongoDB

## notification
patternHint: Trigger → Condition → Send (Email/Slack/SMS)

Suggested nodes:
- n8n-nodes-base.webhook
  displayName: Webhook
  description: Starts the workflow when a webhook is called
  note: Event-based notifications from external systems
- n8n-nodes-base.scheduleTrigger
  displayName: Schedule Trigger
  description: Triggers the workflow on a given schedule
  note: Periodic monitoring and batch notifications
- n8n-nodes-base.gmail
  displayName: Gmail
  description: Consume the Gmail API
  note: Default to this because it's easy for users to setup
- n8n-nodes-base.slack
  displayName: Slack
  description: Consume Slack API
- n8n-nodes-base.telegram
  displayName: Telegram
  description: Sends data to Telegram
- n8n-nodes-base.twilio
  displayName: Twilio
  description: Send SMS and WhatsApp messages or make phone calls
- n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest
  displayName: HTTP Request
  description: Makes an HTTP request and returns the response data
  note: For services without dedicated nodes (Teams, Discord)
- n8n-nodes-base.if
  displayName: If
  description: Route items to different branches (true/false)
  note: Check alert conditions before sending
- n8n-nodes-base.switch
  displayName: Switch
  description: Route items depending on defined expression or rules
  note: If routing by severity/type is needed, use Switch to direct to different channels

## content_generation
patternHint: Trigger → Generate (Text/Image/Video) → Deliver

Suggested nodes:
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.agent
  displayName: AI Agent
  description: Generates an action plan and executes it. Can use external tools.
  note: For text generation
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.openAi
  displayName: OpenAI
  description: Message an assistant or GPT, analyze images, generate audio, etc.
  note: Use for image/video generation. DALL-E, TTS, Sora video generation
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.lmChatGoogleGemini
  displayName: Google Gemini Chat Model
  description: Chat Model Google Gemini
  note: Imagen, video generation
- n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest
  displayName: HTTP Request
  description: Makes an HTTP request and returns the response data
  note: For APIs without dedicated nodes
- n8n-nodes-base.editImage
  displayName: Edit Image
  description: Edits an image like blur, resize or adding border and text
  note: Resize, crop, format conversion
- n8n-nodes-base.markdown
  displayName: Markdown
  description: Convert data between Markdown and HTML
  note: Convert to HTML
- n8n-nodes-base.facebookGraphApi
  displayName: Facebook Graph API
  description: Interacts with Facebook using the Graph API
- n8n-nodes-base.wait
  displayName: Wait
  description: Wait before continue with execution
  note: Video generation is async, use wait while polling for updated

## scraping_and_research
patternHint: Trigger → Fetch → Extract → Store

Suggested nodes:
- n8n-nodes-base.dataTable
  displayName: Data table
  description: Permanently save data across workflow executions in a table
  note: Default storage for scraped data when the user does not specify a destination. No external config needed. Always include a storage step in scraping workflows
- n8n-nodes-base.phantombuster
  displayName: Phantombuster
  description: Consume Phantombuster API
  note: Use this for social media requests: LinkedIn, Facebook, Instagram, Twitter, etc.
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.toolSerpApi
  displayName: SerpApi (Google Search)
  description: Search in Google using SerpAPI
  note: Give agent web search capability, get up-to-date information from websites.
- n8n-nodes-base.perplexity
  displayName: Perplexity
  description:  AI-powered answer engine that provides accurate, trusted, and real-time answers to any question. Generates AI responses with citations
  note: Recommended for fetching up-to-date news
- n8n-nodes-base.perplexityTool
  displayName: Perplexity Tool
  description:  AI-powered answer engine that provides accurate, trusted, and real-time answers to any question. Generates AI responses with citations
  note: Recommended for fetching up-to-date news
- n8n-nodes-base.htmlExtract
  displayName: (not found)
  description: (not found)
  note: Use to extract HTML content from http requests. Though, JS-rendered sites may return empty
- n8n-nodes-base.splitInBatches
  displayName: Loop Over Items (Split in Batches)
  description: Split data into batches and iterate over each batch
  note: Use to batch the processing of items. General recommendation: 200 rows at a time if processing is fast
- n8n-nodes-base.wait
  displayName: Wait
  description: Wait before continue with execution
  note: Use this to avoid rate limits (429 errors)
- n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest
  displayName: HTTP Request
  description: Makes an HTTP request and returns the response data
- n8n-nodes-base.httpRequestTool
  displayName: HTTP Request Tool
  description: Makes an HTTP request and returns the response data

}

## "webhook"
Found 5 nodes:

- n8n-nodes-base.webhook [TRIGGER]
  Display Name: Webhook
  Version: 2.1
  Description: Starts the workflow when a webhook is called
  Discriminators: none (use node directly without resource/operation/mode)

- n8n-nodes-base.respondToWebhook [TRIGGER]
  Display Name: Respond to Webhook
  Version: 1.5
  Description: Returns data for Webhook
  @builderHint Only works with webhook node (n8n-nodes-base.webhook) with responseMode set to "responseNode"
  @relatedNodes
    - n8n-nodes-base.webhook: "Required trigger - set responseMode to "responseNode""
  Discriminators: none (use node directly without resource/operation/mode)

- n8n-nodes-base.flowTrigger [TRIGGER]
  Display Name: Flow Trigger
  Version: 1
  Description: Handle Flow events via webhooks
  Discriminators:
    resource:
      - list:
          operations:
      - task:
          operations:

  Use get_node_types with discriminators:
    get_node_types({ nodeIds: [{ nodeId: "n8n-nodes-base.flowTrigger", resource: "list", operation: "get" }] })

- n8n-nodes-base.taigaTrigger [TRIGGER]
  Display Name: Taiga Trigger
  Version: 1
  Description: Handle Taiga events via webhook
  Discriminators: none (use node directly without resource/operation/mode)

- n8n-nodes-base.wiseTrigger [TRIGGER]
  Display Name: Wise Trigger
  Version: 1
  Description: Handle Wise events via webhooks
  Discriminators: none (use node directly without resource/operation/mode)

## "ai agent"
Found 5 nodes:

- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.agent
  Display Name: AI Agent
  Version: 3.1
  Description: Generates an action plan and executes it. Can use external tools.
  @relatedNodes
    - n8n-nodes-base.aggregate: "Use to combine multiple items together before the agent"
    - @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.outputParserStructured: "Attach for structured output; reference fields as $json.output.fieldName for use in subsequent nodes (conditions, storing data)"
    - @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.agentTool: "For multi-agent systems using orchestrator pattern"
    - @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.memoryBufferWindow: "Required for conversational workflows - connect memory to every agent that needs to recall previous messages in the conversation"
  Discriminators: none (use node directly without resource/operation/mode)

- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.agentTool
  Display Name: AI Agent Tool
  Version: 3
  Description: Generates an action plan and executes it. Can use external tools.
  Discriminators: none (use node directly without resource/operation/mode)

- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.toolCalculator
  Display Name: Calculator

  Discriminators: none (use node directly without resource/operation/mode)

- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.toolThink
  Display Name: Think Tool

  Discriminators: none (use node directly without resource/operation/mode)

- n8n-nodes-base.debugHelper
  Display Name: DebugHelper
  Version: 1
  Description: Causes problems intentionally and generates useful data for debugging
  Discriminators: none (use node directly without resource/operation/mode)

## "openai"
Found 5 nodes:

- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.openAi
  Display Name: OpenAI
  Version: 2.1
  Description: Message an assistant or GPT, analyze images, generate audio, etc.
  @builderHint For text generation, reasoning and tools, use AI Agent with OpenAI Chat Model. This OpenAI node is for specialized operations: image generation (DALL-E), audio (Whisper, TTS), and video generation (Sora).
  @relatedNodes
    - @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.agent: "Prefer for most LLM tasks"
    - @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.lmChatOpenAi: "Prefer for most LLM tasks"
  Discriminators:
    resource:
      - text:
          @builderHint For text generation, reasoning and tools, use AI Agent with OpenAI Chat Model instead of this resource.
          operations:
            - response
              Generate a model response with GPT 3, 4, 5, etc. using Responses API
            - classify
              Check whether content complies with usage policies
      - image:
          operations:
            - analyze
              Take in images and answer questions about them
            - generate
              Creates an image from a text prompt
            - edit
              Edit an image
      - audio:
          operations:
            - generate
              Creates audio from a text prompt
            - transcribe
              Transcribes audio into text
            - translate
              Translates audio into text in English
      - file:
          operations:
            - deleteFile
              Delete a file from the server
            - list
              Returns a list of files that belong to the user's organization
            - upload
              Upload a file that can be used across various endpoints
      - conversation:
          operations:
            - create
              Create a conversation
            - get
              Get a conversation
            - remove
              Remove a conversation
            - update
              Update a conversation
      - video:
          operations:
            - generate
              Creates a video from a text prompt

  Use get_node_types with discriminators:
    get_node_types({ nodeIds: [{ nodeId: "@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.openAi", resource: "text", operation: "response" }] })

- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.lmChatOpenAi
  Display Name: OpenAI Chat Model
  Version: 1.3
  Description: For advanced usage with an AI chain
  Discriminators: none (use node directly without resource/operation/mode)

- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.lmChatAzureOpenAi
  Display Name: Azure OpenAI Chat Model
  Version: 1
  Description: For advanced usage with an AI chain
  Discriminators: none (use node directly without resource/operation/mode)

- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.embeddingsOpenAi
  Display Name: Embeddings OpenAI
  Version: 1.2
  Description: Use Embeddings OpenAI
  Discriminators: none (use node directly without resource/operation/mode)

- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.embeddingsAzureOpenAi
  Display Name: Embeddings Azure OpenAI
  Version: 1
  Description: Use Embeddings Azure OpenAI
  Discriminators: none (use node directly without resource/operation/mode)

## "http request tool"
Found 1 nodes:

- n8n-nodes-base.httpRequestTool
  Display Name: HTTP Request Tool
  Version: 4.4
  Description: Makes an HTTP request and returns the response data
  @builderHint Prefer dedicated integration nodes over HTTP Request — n8n has 400+ dedicated nodes (e.g. Gmail, Slack, Google Sheets, Notion, OpenAI, HubSpot, Jira, etc.) with built-in authentication, pre-configured parameters, better error handling, and easier maintenance. Only use HTTP Request when no dedicated node exists for the service, the user explicitly requests it, accessing a custom/internal API, or the dedicated node does not support the specific operation needed.
  Discriminators: none (use node directly without resource/operation/mode)

---

## "slack"
Found 5 nodes:

- n8n-nodes-base.slack
  Display Name: Slack
  Version: 2.4
  Description: Consume Slack API
  Discriminators:
    resource:
      - channel:
          operations:
            - archive
              Archives a conversation
            - close
              Closes a direct message or multi-person direct message
            - create
              Initiates a public or private channel-based conversation
            - get
              Get information about a channel
            - getAll
              Get many channels in a Slack team
            - history
              Get a conversation's history of messages and events
            - invite
              Invite a user to a channel
            - join
              Joins an existing conversation
            - kick
              Removes a user from a channel
            - leave
              Leaves a conversation
            - member
              List members of a conversation
            - open
              Opens or resumes a direct message or multi-person direct message
            - rename
              Renames a conversation
            - replies
              Get a thread of messages posted to a channel
            - setPurpose
              Sets the purpose for a conversation
            - setTopic
              Sets the topic for a conversation
            - unarchive
              Unarchives a conversation
      - file:
          operations:
            - get
            - getAll
              Get & filters team files
            - upload
              Create or upload an existing file
      - message:
          operations:
            - delete
            - getPermalink
            - search
            - post
            - sendAndWait
            - update
      - reaction:
          operations:
            - add
              Adds a reaction to a message
            - get
              Get the reactions of a message
            - remove
              Remove a reaction of a message
      - star:
          operations:
            - add
              Add a star to an item
            - delete
              Delete a star from an item
            - getAll
              Get many stars of autenticated user
      - user:
          operations:
            - info
              Get information about a user
            - getAll
              Get a list of many users
            - getProfile
              Get a user's profile
            - getPresence
              Get online status of a user
            - updateProfile
              Update a user's profile
      - userGroup:
          operations:
            - updateUsers
            - create
            - disable
            - enable
            - getAll
            - getUsers
            - update

  Use get_node_types with discriminators:
    get_node_types({ nodeIds: [{ nodeId: "n8n-nodes-base.slack", resource: "channel", operation: "archive" }] })

- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.vectorStorePGVector
  Display Name: Postgres PGVector Store
  Version: 1.3
  Description: Work with your data in Postgresql with the PGVector extension
  Discriminators:
    mode:
      - load: "Get Many" → use node()
        Get many ranked documents from vector store for query
      - insert: "Insert Documents" → use node()
        Insert documents into vector store
      - retrieve: "Retrieve Documents (As Vector Store for Chain)" → use vectorStore({ mode: 'retrieve' }) for subnodes.vectorStore
        Retrieve documents from vector store to be used as vector store with AI nodes
      - retrieve-as-tool: "Retrieve Documents (As Tool for AI Agent)" → use tool({ mode: 'retrieve-as-tool' }) for subnodes.tools
        Retrieve documents from vector store to be used as tool with AI nodes

  Use get_node_types with discriminators:
    get_node_types({ nodeIds: [{ nodeId: "@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.vectorStorePGVector", mode: "load" }] })

## "merge"
Found 5 nodes:

- n8n-nodes-base.merge
  Display Name: Merge
  Version: 3.2
  Description: Merges data of multiple streams once data from both is available
  Discriminators:
    mode:
      - append: "Append"
        Output items of each input, one after the other
        @builderHint Append items from multiple branches into a single list sequentially. Waits for all running branches. Supports any number of inputs. @example input1: [{ x }] [{ y }] input2: [{ z }]. Output: [{ x }, { y }, { z }]. Next node will execute 3 times with each item. Set executeOnce on next node to execute once.
      - combine: "Combine"
        Merge matching items together
        @builderHint Combines items from 2 branches. Waits for both to have input data. @example **combine by position** input1: [{ x }, { y }] input2: [{ z }] output: [{ x, y }, { x: undefined, y: undefined, z }] @example **combine by key** input1: [{ id: 1, x }, { id: 2, y }] input2: [{ id: 1, z }] output: [{ id: 1, x, z }, { id: 2, y }]
      - combineBySql: "SQL Query"
        Write a query to do the merge
        @builderHint Need to combine more than 2 branches? Use SQL Query for advanced operations. Waits for all inputs. @example Results depend on query - can filter, join, aggregate
      - chooseBranch: "Choose Branch"
        Output data from a specific branch, without modifying it
        @builderHint Do you need to select data from only ONE specific input and discard the others? Use Choose Branch after conditional nodes to pick which path to continue. Waits for all inputs. @example 3 items from Input A + 2 items from Input B, choose Input A → 3 items

  Use get_node_types with discriminators:
    get_node_types({ nodeIds: [{ nodeId: "n8n-nodes-base.merge", mode: "append" }] })

# TypeScript Type Definitions

## n8n-nodes-base.webhook (v21)

```typescript
/**
 * Webhook Node - Version 2.1
 * Starts the workflow when a webhook is called
 */


export interface WebhookV21Params {
/**
 * Whether to allow the webhook to listen for multiple HTTP methods
 * @default false
 */
    multipleMethods?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
/**
 * The HTTP method to listen to
 * @displayOptions.show { multipleMethods: [false, true] }
 * @default GET
 */
    httpMethod?: 'DELETE' | 'GET' | 'HEAD' | 'PATCH' | 'POST' | 'PUT' | Expression<string>;
/**
 * The path to listen to, dynamic values could be specified by using ':', e.g. 'your-path/:dynamic-value'. If dynamic values are set 'webhookId' would be prepended to path.
 * @builderHint The webhook path that triggers this workflow
 */
    path?: string | Expression<string>;
/**
 * The way to authenticate
 * @default none
 */
    authentication?: 'basicAuth' | 'headerAuth' | 'jwtAuth' | 'none' | Expression<string>;
/**
 * When and how to respond to the webhook
 * @builderHint Use 'responseNode' to respond via a 'Respond to Webhook' node later in the workflow
 * @default onReceived
 */
    responseMode?: 'onReceived' | 'lastNode' | 'responseNode' | 'streaming' | Expression<string>;
/**
 * What data should be returned. If it should return all items as an array or only the first item as object.
 * @displayOptions.show { responseMode: ["lastNode"] }
 * @default firstEntryJson
 */
    responseData?: 'allEntries' | 'firstEntryJson' | 'firstEntryBinary' | 'noData' | Expression<string>;
/**
 * Name of the binary property to return
 * @displayOptions.show { responseData: ["firstEntryBinary"] }
 * @default data
 */
    responseBinaryPropertyName?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
  options?: {
    /** Comma-separated list of URLs allowed for cross-origin non-preflight requests. Use * (default) to allow all origins.
     * @default *
     */
    allowedOrigins?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
    /** Whether the webhook will receive binary data
     * @displayOptions.show { /httpMethod: ["PATCH", "PUT", "POST"] }
     * @default false
     */
    binaryData?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
    /** The name of the output field to put any binary file data in. Only relevant if binary data is received.
     * @default data
     */
    binaryPropertyName?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
    /** Whether to ignore requests from bots like link previewers and web crawlers
     * @default false
     */
    ignoreBots?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
    /** Comma-separated list of allowed IP addresses or CIDR ranges. Leave empty to allow all IPs.
     */
    ipWhitelist?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
    /** Whether to send any body in the response
     * @displayOptions.show { /responseMode: ["onReceived"] }
     * @displayOptions.hide { rawBody: [true] }
     * @default false
     */
    noResponseBody?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
    /** Name of the property to return the data of instead of the whole JSON
     * @displayOptions.show { /responseData: ["firstEntryJson"], /responseMode: ["lastNode"] }
     * @default data
     */
    responsePropertyName?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
    /** If the data gets received via "Form-Data Multipart" it will be the prefix and a number starting with 0 will be attached to it
     * @hint The name of the output binary field to put the file in
     * @displayOptions.show { binaryData: [true] }
     * @default data
     */
    binaryPropertyName?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
    /** Raw body (binary)
     * @displayOptions.hide { binaryData: [true], noResponseBody: [true] }
     * @default false
     */
    rawBody?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
    /** Whether to return the raw body
     * @displayOptions.hide { noResponseBody: [true] }
     * @default false
     */
    rawBody?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
    /** Response Code
     * @displayOptions.hide { /responseMode: ["responseNode"] }
     * @default {"values":{"responseCode":200}}
     */
    responseCode?: {
        /** Values
     */
    values?: {
      /** The HTTP response code to return
       * @default 200
       */
      responseCode?: 200 | 201 | 204 | 301 | 302 | 304 | 400 | 401 | 403 | 404 | 'customCode' | Expression<number>;
      /** Code
       * @displayOptions.show { responseCode: ["customCode"] }
       * @default 200
       */
      customCode?: number | Expression<number>;
    };
  };
    /** Set a custom content-type to return if another one as the "application/json" should be returned
     * @displayOptions.show { /responseData: ["firstEntryJson"], /responseMode: ["lastNode"] }
     */
    responseContentType?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
    /** Custom response data to send
     * @displayOptions.show { /responseMode: ["onReceived"] }
     * @displayOptions.hide { noResponseBody: [true] }
     */
    responseData?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
    /** Add headers to the webhook response
     * @default {}
     */
    responseHeaders?: {
        /** Entries
     */
    entries?: Array<{
      /** Name of the header
       */
      name?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
      /** Value of the header
       */
      value?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
    }>;
  };
  };
}

export type WebhookV21Output = {
  headers?: Record<string, unknown>;
  params?: Record<string, unknown>;
  query?: Record<string, unknown>;
  body?: Record<string, unknown>;
  webhookUrl?: string;
  executionMode?: string;
};

export interface WebhookV21Credentials {
  httpBasicAuth: CredentialReference;
  httpHeaderAuth: CredentialReference;
  jwtAuth: CredentialReference;
}

interface WebhookV21NodeBase {
  type: 'n8n-nodes-base.webhook';
  version: 2.1;
  credentials?: WebhookV21Credentials;
  isTrigger: true;
}

export type WebhookV21ParamsNode = WebhookV21NodeBase & {
  config: NodeConfig<WebhookV21Params>;
  output?: Items<WebhookV21Output>;
};

export type WebhookV21Node = WebhookV21ParamsNode;
```

---

## @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.agent (v31)

```typescript
/**
 * AI Agent Node - Version 3.1
 * Generates an action plan and executes it. Can use external tools.
 */


export interface LcAgentV31Params {
  aiAgentStarterCallout?: unknown;
/**
 * Source for Prompt (User Message)
 * @builderHint Use 'auto' when following a chat trigger, 'define' when custom prompt needed
 * @default auto
 */
    promptType?: 'auto' | 'define' | Expression<string>;
/**
 * Prompt (User Message)
 * @displayOptions.show { promptType: ["guardrails", "auto", "define"] }
 * @default ={{ $json.guardrailsInput }}
 */
    text?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
  hasOutputParser?: boolean;
  needsFallback?: boolean;
  options?: {
    /** The message that will be sent to the agent before the conversation starts
     * @builderHint Must include: agent's purpose, exact names of connected tools, and response instructions
     * @default You are a helpful assistant
     */
    systemMessage?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
    /** The maximum number of iterations the agent will run before stopping
     * @default 10
     */
    maxIterations?: number | Expression<number>;
    /** Whether or not the output should include intermediate steps the agent took
     * @default false
     */
    returnIntermediateSteps?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
    /** Whether or not binary images should be automatically passed through to the agent as image type messages
     * @default true
     */
    passthroughBinaryImages?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
    /** Whether this agent will stream the response in real-time as it generates text
     * @default true
     */
    enableStreaming?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
    /** Batch processing options for rate limiting
     * @default {}
     */
    batching?: {
    /** How many items to process in parallel. This is useful for rate limiting, but might impact the log output ordering.
     * @default 1
     */
    batchSize?: number | Expression<number>;
    /** Delay in milliseconds between batches. This is useful for rate limiting.
     * @default 0
     */
    delayBetweenBatches?: number | Expression<number>;
  };
    /** The maximum number of tokens to read from the chat memory history. Set to 0 to read all history.
     * @default 0
     */
    maxTokensFromMemory?: unknown;
  };
}

export interface LcAgentV31SubnodeConfig {
  model: LanguageModelInstance | LanguageModelInstance[];
  memory?: MemoryInstance;
  tools?: ToolInstance[];
  /**
   * @displayOptions.show { hasOutputParser: [true] }
   */
  outputParser?: OutputParserInstance;
}

interface LcAgentV31NodeBase {
  type: '@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.agent';
  version: 3.1;
}

export type LcAgentV31ParamsNode = LcAgentV31NodeBase & {
  config: NodeConfig<LcAgentV31Params> & { subnodes: LcAgentV31SubnodeConfig };
};

export type LcAgentV31Node = LcAgentV31ParamsNode;
```

## @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.lmChatOpenAi (v13)

```typescript
/**
 * OpenAI Chat Model Node - Version 1.3
 * For advanced usage with an AI chain
 */


export interface LcLmChatOpenAiV13Params {
/**
 * The model. Choose from the list, or specify an ID.
 * @builderHint Always default to latest mini model gpt-5-mini
 * @default {"mode":"list","value":"gpt-5-mini"}
 */
    model?: { __rl: true; mode: 'list' | 'id'; value: string; cachedResultName?: string };
/**
 * Whether to use the Responses API to generate the response. &lt;a href="https://docs.n8n.io/integrations/builtin/cluster-nodes/sub-nodes/n8n-nodes-langchain.lmchatopenai/#use-responses-api"&gt;Learn more&lt;/a&gt;.
 * @default true
 */
    responsesApiEnabled?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
/**
 * Built-in Tools
 * @displayOptions.show { /responsesApiEnabled: [true] }
 * @default {}
 */
    builtInTools?: {
    /** Web Search
     * @default {"searchContextSize":"medium"}
     */
    webSearch?: {
    /** High level guidance for the amount of context window space to use for the search
     * @default medium
     */
    searchContextSize?: 'low' | 'medium' | 'high' | Expression<string>;
    /** Comma-separated list of domains to search. Only domains in this list will be searched.
     */
    allowedDomains?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
    /** Country
     */
    country?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
    /** City
     */
    city?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
    /** Region
     */
    region?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
  };
    /** File Search
     * @default {"vectorStoreIds":"[]"}
     */
    fileSearch?: {
    /** The vector store IDs to use for the file search. Vector stores are managed via OpenAI Dashboard. &lt;a href="https://docs.n8n.io/integrations/builtin/cluster-nodes/sub-nodes/n8n-nodes-langchain.lmchatopenai/#built-in-tools"&gt;Learn more&lt;/a&gt;.
     * @default []
     */
    vectorStoreIds?: IDataObject | string | Expression<string>;
    /** Filters
     * @default {}
     */
    filters?: IDataObject | string | Expression<string>;
    /** Max Results
     * @default 1
     */
    maxResults?: number | Expression<number>;
  };
    /** Whether to allow the model to execute code in a sandboxed environment
     * @default true
     */
    codeInterpreter?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
  };
/**
 * Additional options to add
 * @default {}
 */
    options?: {
    /** Override the default base URL for the API
     * @default https://api.openai.com/v1
     */
    baseURL?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
    /** Positive values penalize new tokens based on their existing frequency in the text so far, decreasing the model's likelihood to repeat the same line verbatim
     * @default 0
     */
    frequencyPenalty?: number | Expression<number>;
    /** The maximum number of tokens to generate in the completion. Most models have a context length of 2048 tokens (except for the newest models, which support 32,768).
     * @default -1
     */
    maxTokens?: number | Expression<number>;
    /** Response Format
     * @default text
     */
    responseFormat?: 'text' | 'json_object' | Expression<string>;
    /** Response Format
     * @displayOptions.show { /responsesApiEnabled: [false] }
     * @default text
     */
    responseFormat?: 'text' | 'json_object' | Expression<string>;
    /** Response Format
     * @displayOptions.show { /responsesApiEnabled: [true] }
     * @default {"textOptions":[{"type":"text"}]}
     */
    textFormat?: {
        /** Text
     */
    textOptions?: {
      /** Type
       */
      type?: 'text' | 'json_schema' | 'json_object' | Expression<string>;
      /** Verbosity
       * @default medium
       */
      verbosity?: 'low' | 'medium' | 'high' | Expression<string>;
      /** The name of the response format. Must be a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or contain underscores and dashes, with a maximum length of 64.
       * @displayOptions.show { type: ["json_schema"] }
       * @default my_schema
       */
      name?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
      /** The schema of the response format
       * @displayOptions.show { type: ["json_schema"] }
       */
      schema?: IDataObject | string | Expression<string>;
      /** The description of the response format
       * @displayOptions.show { type: ["json_schema"] }
       */
      description?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
      /** Whether to require that the AI will always generate responses that match the provided JSON Schema
       * @displayOptions.show { type: ["json_schema"] }
       * @default false
       */
      strict?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
    };
  };
    /** Positive values penalize new tokens based on whether they appear in the text so far, increasing the model's likelihood to talk about new topics
     * @default 0
     */
    presencePenalty?: number | Expression<number>;
    /** Controls randomness: Lowering results in less random completions. As the temperature approaches zero, the model will become deterministic and repetitive.
     * @default 0.7
     */
    temperature?: number | Expression<number>;
    /** Controls the amount of reasoning tokens to use. A value of "low" will favor speed and economical token usage, "high" will favor more complete reasoning at the cost of more tokens generated and slower responses.
     * @displayOptions.show { /model: [{"_cnd":{"regex":"(^o1([-\\d]+)?$)|(^o[3-9].*)|(^gpt-5.*)"}}] }
     * @default medium
     */
    reasoningEffort?: 'low' | 'medium' | 'high' | Expression<string>;
    /** Maximum amount of time a request is allowed to take in milliseconds
     * @default 60000
     */
    timeout?: number | Expression<number>;
    /** Maximum number of retries to attempt
     * @default 2
     */
    maxRetries?: number | Expression<number>;
    /** Controls diversity via nucleus sampling: 0.5 means half of all likelihood-weighted options are considered. We generally recommend altering this or temperature but not both.
     * @default 1
     */
    topP?: number | Expression<number>;
    /** The conversation that this response belongs to. Input items and output items from this response are automatically added to this conversation after this response completes.
     * @displayOptions.show { /responsesApiEnabled: [true] }
     */
    conversationId?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
    /** Used by OpenAI to cache responses for similar requests to optimize your cache hit rates
     * @displayOptions.show { /responsesApiEnabled: [true] }
     */
    promptCacheKey?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
    /** A stable identifier used to help detect users of your application that may be violating OpenAI's usage policies. The IDs should be a string that uniquely identifies each user.
     * @displayOptions.show { /responsesApiEnabled: [true] }
     */
    safetyIdentifier?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
    /** The service tier to use for the request
     * @displayOptions.show { /responsesApiEnabled: [true] }
     * @default auto
     */
    serviceTier?: 'auto' | 'flex' | 'default' | 'priority' | Expression<string>;
    /** Set of 16 key-value pairs that can be attached to an object. This can be useful for storing additional information about the object in a structured format, and querying for objects via API or the dashboard. Keys are strings with a maximum length of 64 characters. Values are strings with a maximum length of 512 characters.
     * @displayOptions.show { /responsesApiEnabled: [true] }
     * @default {}
     */
    metadata?: IDataObject | string | Expression<string>;
    /** An integer between 0 and 20 specifying the number of most likely tokens to return at each token position, each with an associated log probability
     * @displayOptions.show { /responsesApiEnabled: [true] }
     * @default 0
     */
    topLogprobs?: number | Expression<number>;
    /** Configure the reusable prompt template configured via OpenAI Dashboard. &lt;a href="https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/prompt-engineering#reusable-prompts"&gt;Learn more&lt;/a&gt;.
     * @displayOptions.show { /responsesApiEnabled: [true] }
     * @default {"promptOptions":[{"promptId":""}]}
     */
    promptConfig?: {
        /** Prompt
     */
    promptOptions?: {
      /** The unique identifier of the prompt template to use
       */
      promptId?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
      /** Optional version of the prompt template
       */
      version?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
      /** Variables to be substituted into the prompt template
       * @default {}
       */
      variables?: IDataObject | string | Expression<string>;
    };
  };
  };
}

export interface LcLmChatOpenAiV13Credentials {
  openAiApi: CredentialReference;
}

interface LcLmChatOpenAiV13NodeBase {
  type: '@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.lmChatOpenAi';
  version: 1.3;
  credentials?: LcLmChatOpenAiV13Credentials;
  isTrigger: true;
}

export type LcLmChatOpenAiV13ParamsNode = LcLmChatOpenAiV13NodeBase & {
  config: NodeConfig<LcLmChatOpenAiV13Params>;
};

export type LcLmChatOpenAiV13Node = LcLmChatOpenAiV13ParamsNode;
```

## @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.toolSerpApi (v1)

```typescript
/**
 * SerpApi (Google Search) Node - Version 1
 * Search in Google using SerpAPI
 */


export interface LcToolSerpApiV1Params {
  options?: {
    /** Defines the country to use for search. Head to &lt;a href="https://serpapi.com/google-countries"&gt;Google countries page&lt;/a&gt; for a full list of supported countries.
     * @default us
     */
    gl?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
    /** Device to use to get the results
     * @default desktop
     */
    device?: 'desktop' | 'mobile' | 'tablet' | Expression<string>;
    /** Whether to force SerpApi to fetch the Google results even if a cached version is already present. Cache expires after 1h. Cached searches are free, and are not counted towards your searches per month.
     * @default false
     */
    no_cache?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
    /** Defines the domain to use for search. Head to &lt;a href="https://serpapi.com/google-domains"&gt;Google domains page&lt;/a&gt; for a full list of supported domains.
     * @default google.com
     */
    google_domain?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
    /** Defines the language to use. It's a two-letter language code. (e.g., `en` for English, `es` for Spanish, or `fr` for French). Head to &lt;a href="https://serpapi.com/google-languages"&gt;Google languages page&lt;/a&gt; for a full list of supported languages.
     * @default en
     */
    hl?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
  };
}

export interface LcToolSerpApiV1Credentials {
  serpApi: CredentialReference;
}

interface LcToolSerpApiV1NodeBase {
  type: '@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.toolSerpApi';
  version: 1;
  credentials?: LcToolSerpApiV1Credentials;
  isTrigger: true;
}

export type LcToolSerpApiV1ParamsNode = LcToolSerpApiV1NodeBase & {
  config: NodeConfig<LcToolSerpApiV1Params>;
};

export type LcToolSerpApiV1Node = LcToolSerpApiV1ParamsNode;
```
