      "parameters": {
        "promptType": "define",
        "text": "={{ \"Create 3 social media post variations based on this content plan: \" + JSON.stringify($(\"Strategy Agent\").item.json.output) + \". Use Twitter Formatting and LinkedIn Formatting tools for platform-specific formatting. Use Hashtag Generation tool to create relevant hashtags. Produce 3 distinct variations.\" }}",
        "hasOutputParser": true,
        "options": {
          "systemMessage": "You are a social media content specialist. Your role is to create engaging, platform-optimized social media posts. Use formatting and hashtag tools to produce multiple variations tailored to Twitter and LinkedIn audiences."
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "48192961-f04d-463f-915d-49c156efcde8",
      "name": "Twitter Formatting",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.httpRequestTool",
      "typeVersion": 4.4,
      "position": [
        2600,
        1400
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "method": "POST",
        "url": "<__PLACEHOLDER_VALUE__Your social media formatting API URL__>",
        "authentication": "none",
        "sendBody": true,
        "contentType": "json",
        "specifyBody": "json",
        "jsonBody": "={{ { \"platform\": \"twitter\", \"content\": $json.content } }}"
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "4ca45fd0-b293-4cac-91ea-29093ea30072",
      "name": "LinkedIn Formatting",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.httpRequestTool",
      "typeVersion": 4.4,
      "position": [
        2800,
        1400
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "method": "POST",
        "url": "<__PLACEHOLDER_VALUE__Your social media formatting API URL__>",
        "authentication": "none",
        "sendBody": true,
        "contentType": "json",
        "specifyBody": "json",
        "jsonBody": "={{ { \"platform\": \"linkedin\", \"content\": $json.content } }}"
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "0a8572e0-9c71-4999-a92c-326f7a212ab5",
      "name": "Hashtag Generation",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.httpRequestTool",
      "typeVersion": 4.4,
      "position": [
        3000,
        1400
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "method": "POST",
        "url": "<__PLACEHOLDER_VALUE__Your hashtag generation API URL__>",
        "authentication": "none",
        "sendBody": true,
        "contentType": "json",
        "specifyBody": "json",
        "jsonBody": "={{ { \"topic\": $json.topic, \"platform\": $json.platform } }}"
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "4d42f67c-dcf1-4ca5-b1ab-257a66f2b3f6",
      "name": "Social Output Parser",
      "type": "@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.outputParserStructured",
      "typeVersion": 1.3,
      "position": [
        4200,
        1400
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "schemaType": "fromJson",
        "jsonSchemaExample": "{ \"variations\": [{ \"platform\": \"twitter\", \"content\": \"post text\", \"hashtags\": [\"tag1\"] }, { \"platform\": \"linkedin\", \"content\": \"post text\", \"hashtags\": [\"tag1\"] }, { \"platform\": \"twitter\", \"content\": \"variation 2\", \"hashtags\": [\"tag2\"] }] }"
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "de055e7d-cea3-4b63-b412-8c8686a595f4",
      "name": "Editorial Review Agent",
      "type": "@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.agent",
      "typeVersion": 3.1,
      "position": [
        2040,
        600
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "promptType": "define",
        "text": "={{ \"Review this content: \" + JSON.stringify($json.output) + \". Use Grammar Checking tool to identify grammar issues and Fact Verification tool to verify claims. Provide a quality score (0-100), list grammar issues, fact-check results, and revision notes if score is below 85.\" }}",
        "hasOutputParser": true,
        "options": {
          "systemMessage": "You are an editorial reviewer. Your role is to assess content quality using grammar checking and fact verification tools. Provide objective quality scores (0-100) and detailed revision notes for content scoring below 85."
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "27a0ba2b-695b-49b6-8961-46b200672d66",
      "name": "Grammar Checking",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.httpRequestTool",
      "typeVersion": 4.4,
      "position": [
        3200,
        1400
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "method": "POST",
        "url": "<__PLACEHOLDER_VALUE__Your grammar checking API URL (e.g., Grammarly, LanguageTool)__>",
        "authentication": "genericCredentialType",
        "genericAuthType": "httpHeaderAuth",
        "sendBody": true,
        "contentType": "json",
        "specifyBody": "json",
        "jsonBody": "={{ { \"text\": $json.content } }}"
      },
      "credentials": {}
    },
    {
      "id": "d4dcaaef-cab3-43f0-8b65-f15247a62b28",
      "name": "Fact Verification",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.httpRequestTool",
      "typeVersion": 4.4,
      "position": [
        3400,
        1400
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "method": "POST",
        "url": "<__PLACEHOLDER_VALUE__Your fact-checking API URL__>",
        "authentication": "genericCredentialType",
        "genericAuthType": "httpHeaderAuth",
        "sendBody": true,
        "contentType": "json",
        "specifyBody": "json",
        "jsonBody": "={{ { \"content\": $json.content, \"sources\": $json.sources } }}"
      },
      "credentials": {}
    },
    {
      "id": "fae45f4b-a067-48d4-ba06-6c4c233ceb47",
      "name": "Editorial Output Parser",
      "type": "@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.outputParserStructured",
      "typeVersion": 1.3,
      "position": [
        4400,
        1400
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "schemaType": "fromJson",
        "jsonSchemaExample": "{ \"quality_score\": 92, \"grammar_issues\": [\"issue1\"], \"fact_check_results\": [\"verified\"], \"revision_notes\": \"suggestions for improvement\" }"
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "e2d19cf0-c76f-4381-ba6b-b245d1a3fe71",
      "name": "Check Quality Score",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.if",
      "typeVersion": 2.3,
      "position": [
        2340,
        600
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "conditions": {
          "conditions": [
            {
              "leftValue": "={{ $json.output.quality_score }}",
              "rightValue": 85,
              "operator": {
                "type": "number",
                "operation": "lt"
              }
            }
          ]
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "7ded1a72-1f96-442b-bb69-9e426e088e55",
      "name": "Prepare Approved Content",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.set",
      "typeVersion": 3.4,
      "position": [
        2640,
        500
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "mode": "manual",
        "assignments": {
          "assignments": [
            {
              "id": "1",
              "name": "content",
              "value": "={{ $(\"Editorial Review Agent\").item.json.output }}",
              "type": "object"
            },
            {
              "id": "2",
              "name": "topic",
              "value": "={{ $(\"Content Request Webhook\").item.json.topic }}",
              "type": "string"
            },
            {
              "id": "3",
              "name": "content_type",
              "value": "={{ $(\"Content Request Webhook\").item.json.content_type }}",
              "type": "string"
            },
            {
              "id": "4",
              "name": "quality_score",
              "value": "={{ $(\"Editorial Review Agent\").item.json.output.quality_score }}",
              "type": "number"
            }
          ]
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "dea1ba85-0e83-4a11-bc05-964c497123ae",
      "name": "Merge Approved and Revised",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.merge",
      "typeVersion": 3.2,
      "position": [
        3540,
        600
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "mode": "append"
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "5da6f884-dbaf-49ec-be7d-7b5eed63276d",
      "name": "Prepare Revision Request",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.set",
      "typeVersion": 3.4,
      "position": [
        2640,
        700
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "mode": "manual",
        "assignments": {
          "assignments": [
            {
              "id": "1",
              "name": "revision_notes",
              "value": "={{ $json.output.revision_notes }}",
              "type": "string"
            },
            {
              "id": "2",
              "name": "original_content",
              "value": "={{ $(\"Merge Content Paths\").item.json.output }}",
              "type": "object"
            },
            {
              "id": "3",
              "name": "revision_count",
              "value": "={{ ($json.revision_count || 0) + 1 }}",
              "type": "number"
            }
          ]
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "fd2d4065-5033-45f9-a777-90ce7a1d667f",
      "name": "Check Revision Limit",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.if",
      "typeVersion": 2.3,
      "position": [
        2940,
        700
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "conditions": {
          "conditions": [
            {
              "leftValue": "={{ $json.revision_count }}",
              "rightValue": 3,
              "operator": {
                "type": "number",
                "operation": "lte"
              }
            }
          ]
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "6db69eaa-80fa-4c97-920e-7bc4d35bc0d8",
      "name": "Revise Content",
      "type": "@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.agent",
      "typeVersion": 3.1,
      "position": [
        3240,
        800
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "promptType": "define",
        "text": "={{ \"Revise this content based on the following notes: \" + $json.revision_notes + \". Original content: \" + JSON.stringify($json.original_content) + \". This is revision attempt \" + $json.revision_count + \" of 3.\" }}",
        "hasOutputParser": true,
        "options": {
          "systemMessage": "You are a content revision specialist. Your role is to improve content based on editorial feedback. Address all revision notes while maintaining the original intent and improving quality metrics."
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "1da53a30-b3f8-4ff2-a140-ec1417fb16fa",
      "name": "Max Revisions Reached",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.set",
      "typeVersion": 3.4,
      "position": [
        3240,
        600
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "mode": "manual",
        "assignments": {
          "assignments": [
            {
              "id": "1",
              "name": "status",
              "value": "max_revisions_reached",
              "type": "string"
            },
            {
              "id": "2",
              "name": "message",
              "value": "Maximum revision attempts (3) reached",
              "type": "string"
            },
            {
              "id": "3",
              "name": "content",
              "value": "={{ $(\"Merge Content Paths\").item.json.output }}",
              "type": "object"
            }
          ]
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "b7ab21f7-0c7b-481d-824d-68b3dfddbd23",
      "name": "Send Slack Approval Request",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.slack",
      "typeVersion": 2.4,
      "position": [
        3840,
        600
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "resource": "message",
        "operation": "post",
        "select": "channel",
        "channelId": {
          "__rl": true,
          "mode": "list",
          "value": ""
        },
        "messageType": "text",
        "text": "={{ \"Content ready for approval:\\n\\nTopic: \" + $json.topic + \"\\nType: \" + $json.content_type + \"\\nQuality Score: \" + $json.quality_score + \"\\n\\nContent: \" + JSON.stringify($json.content) + \"\\n\\nApprove at: \" + $execution.resumeUrl }}"
      },
      "credentials": {}
    },
    {
      "id": "1cb7a6af-b077-45e4-8568-1a52669d4459",
      "name": "Wait for Approval",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.wait",
      "typeVersion": 1.1,
      "position": [
        4140,
        600
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "resume": "webhook",
        "httpMethod": "POST",
        "responseMode": "lastNode",
        "limitWaitTime": true,
        "limitType": "afterTimeInterval",
        "resumeAmount": 24,
        "resumeUnit": "hours"
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "56f45a29-3238-4608-82de-c452e3c09664",
      "name": "Check Approval Status",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.if",
      "typeVersion": 2.3,
      "position": [
        4440,
        600
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "conditions": {
          "conditions": [
            {
              "leftValue": "={{ $json.body?.approved }}",
              "rightValue": true,
              "operator": {
                "type": "boolean",
                "operation": "equals"
              }
            }
          ]
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "b147d5b8-f71f-4e1b-9564-70ac34c1aac8",
      "name": "Prepare Google Drive Data",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.set",
      "typeVersion": 3.4,
      "position": [
        4740,
        600
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "mode": "manual",
        "assignments": {
          "assignments": [
            {
              "id": "1",
              "name": "data",
              "value": "={{ JSON.stringify($(\"Merge Approved and Revised\").item.json) }}",
              "type": "string"
            }
          ]
        },
        "options": {
          "includeBinary": true
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "5b91990c-e15e-4e01-ba12-45b26ea9eae3",
      "name": "Archive to Google Drive",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.googleDrive",
      "typeVersion": 3,
      "position": [
        4740,
        700
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "resource": "file",
        "operation": "upload",
        "inputDataFieldName": "data",
        "name": "={{ $(\"Merge Approved and Revised\").item.json.topic + \"_\" + $now.toFormat(\"yyyy-MM-dd\") + \".json\" }}",
        "driveId": {
          "__rl": true,
          "mode": "list",
          "value": "My Drive"
        },
        "folderId": {
          "__rl": true,
          "mode": "list",
          "value": ""
        }
      },
      "credentials": {}
    },
    {
      "id": "ab629b7b-6b7b-4c2f-b342-a9f90490f7e2",
      "name": "Merge Publish Results",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.merge",
      "typeVersion": 3.2,
      "position": [
        5040,
        600
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "mode": "combine",
        "combineBy": "combineByPosition"
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "3a672182-0005-4b76-81dc-1260ba848333",
      "name": "Handle Rejection",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.set",
      "typeVersion": 3.4,
      "position": [
        4740,
        800
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "mode": "manual",
        "assignments": {
          "assignments": [
            {
              "id": "1",
              "name": "status",
              "value": "rejected",
              "type": "string"
            },
            {
              "id": "2",
              "name": "message",
              "value": "Content was not approved",
              "type": "string"
            }
          ]
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "59727408-839f-4d14-9ca3-b32fa85d2d42",
      "name": "Publish to WordPress",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.wordpress",
      "typeVersion": 1,
      "position": [
        4740,
        500
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "resource": "post",
        "operation": "create",
        "title": "={{ $(\"Merge Approved and Revised\").item.json.topic }}",
        "additionalFields": {
          "content": "={{ $(\"Merge Approved and Revised\").item.json.content.content || JSON.stringify($(\"Merge Approved and Revised\").item.json.content) }}",
          "status": "publish"
        }
      },
      "credentials": {}
    },
    {
      "id": "ef1b8d5b-9560-46f6-b890-15ac14231bd0",
      "name": "Store in Airtable",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.airtable",
      "typeVersion": 2.1,
      "position": [
        5340,
        600
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "resource": "record",
        "operation": "create",
        "base": {
          "__rl": true,
          "mode": "list",
          "value": ""
        },
        "table": {
          "__rl": true,
          "mode": "list",
          "value": ""
        },
        "columns": {
          "mappingMode": "defineBelow",
          "value": {
            "topic": "={{ $(\"Merge Approved and Revised\").item.json.topic }}",
            "content_type": "={{ $(\"Merge Approved and Revised\").item.json.content_type }}",
            "quality_score": "={{ $(\"Merge Approved and Revised\").item.json.quality_score }}",
            "wordpress_url": "={{ $(\"Publish to WordPress\").item.json.link }}",
            "google_drive_url": "={{ $(\"Archive to Google Drive\").item.json.webViewLink }}",
            "created_at": "={{ $now.toISO() }}"
          }
        }
      },
      "credentials": {}
    }
  ],
  "connections": {
    "Content Request Webhook": {
      "main": [
        [
          {
            "node": "Research Agent",
            "type": "main",
            "index": 0
          }
        ]
      ]
    },
    "Research Agent": {
      "main": [
        [
          {
            "node": "Strategy Agent",
            "type": "main",
            "index": 0
          }
        ]
      ]
    },
    "OpenAI Model": {
      "ai_languageModel": [
        [
          {
            "node": "Research Agent",
            "type": "ai_languageModel",
            "index": 0
          },
          {
            "node": "Strategy Agent",
            "type": "ai_languageModel",
            "index": 0
          },
          {
            "node": "Writing Agent",

          },
          {

          }
        ]
      ]
    },
    "Web Search": {

          {

            "index": 0
          }
        ]
      ]
    },

      "ai_tool": [
        [
          {

            "index": 0
          }
        ]
      ]
    },
    "Research Output Parser": {
      "ai_outputParser": [
        [
          {

            "index": 0
          }
        ]
      ]
    },
    "Strategy Agent": {

        [
          {

      "ai_tool": [

        ]
      ]
    },

      "ai_tool": [
        [
          {

          {

        [
          {

        [

          }

    },

      "ai_outputParser": [
        [
          {

          {

          }
        ]

    },
    "Social Media Agent": {
      "main": [
        [
          {
            "node": "Merge Content Paths",
            "type": "main",
            "index": 1
          }
        ]
      ]
    },
    "Twitter Formatting": {
      "ai_tool": [
        [
          {

          }
        ]
      ]
    },

      "ai_tool": [

      "ai_tool": [

        ]
      ]
    },
    "Social Output Parser": {
      "ai_outputParser": [

          }

      ]
    },

      "main": [
        [
          {

      "ai_tool": [
        [
          {

      ]
    },

      "ai_tool": [
        [
          {

            "type": "ai_tool",

          }
        ]
      ]
    },
    "Editorial Output Parser": {
      "ai_outputParser": [
        [
          {
            "node": "Editorial Review Agent",
            "type": "ai_outputParser",
            "index": 0
          }
        ]
      ]
    },
    "Check Quality Score": {
      "main": [
        [
          {
            "node": "Prepare Approved Content",
            "type": "main",
            "index": 0
          }
        ],
        [

COMPLEX Create a multi-stage AI pipeline workflow for repurposing YouTube content into s... community api PASS 9/10 (90%) 8 get_suggested_nodes search_nodes search_nodes search_nodes get_node_types str_replace_based_edit_tool batch_str_replace 1.8m 27 6,870 341

Prompt

Create a multi-stage AI pipeline workflow for repurposing YouTube content into short-form clips. First, scrape the YouTube video transcript with timestamps using the Apify integration. Then run four AI processing stages: (1) A title generation step that produces three title variations — SEO-Focused, Curiosity-Driven, and Hybrid — following rules for 40-60 character length and CTR optimization with power words. (2) A thumbnail prompt generation step that transforms rough visual concepts into detailed image generation prompts specifying fonts, colors, and 16:9 aspect ratio. (3) A clip identification step that finds self-contained clips of at least 45 seconds from the transcript with benefit-driven captions and proper start/end boundaries. (4) A description generation step that creates YouTube descriptions with properly formatted chapter timestamps in MM:SS or HH:MM:SS format derived from the transcript. Store all generated outputs in Airtable for coordination.

Workflow Preview

Checklist (9/10)

  • ✓ [structure] Scrapes a YouTube video transcript with timestamps using the Apify integration
    The 'Scrape YouTube Transcript' node uses an HTTP POST request to an Apify Actor API URL, which is explicitly mentioned in the placeholder URL comment. The pinData also confirms transcript with timestamps is retrieved.
  • ✗ [structure] Has four sequential AI processing stages after transcript retrieval: title generation, thumbnail prompt generation, clip identification, and description generation
    The four AI stages (title generation, thumbnail prompt, clip identification, description generation) exist but they run in parallel (all connected from 'Scrape YouTube Transcript' simultaneously), not sequentially. The checklist requires four sequential AI processing stages.
  • ✓ [data] Title generation stage produces exactly three title variations labeled SEO-Focused, Curiosity-Driven, and Hybrid
    The 'Generate Title Variations' prompt explicitly requests exactly three title variations labeled SEO-Focused, Curiosity-Driven, and Hybrid, and the JSON format uses keys seoTitle, curiosityTitle, and hybridTitle corresponding to these labels.
  • ✓ [data] Title generation stage enforces 40-60 character length rules and CTR optimization using power words
    The title generation prompt specifies '40-60 characters' for all three title types and includes power words (Ultimate, Secret, Proven, Essential, Revolutionary, Game-Changing) to maximize CTR.
  • ✓ [data] Thumbnail prompt generation stage transforms rough visual concepts into detailed image generation prompts specifying fonts, colors, and 16:9 aspect ratio
    The 'Generate Thumbnail Prompts' node explicitly instructs transformation of rough visual concepts into detailed image generation prompts, specifying font styles (bold, sans-serif), color scheme, and aspect ratio 16:9 (1920x1080).
  • ✓ [data] Clip identification stage finds self-contained clips of at least 45 seconds from the transcript
    The 'Identify Short-Form Clips' prompt explicitly requires each clip to be 'at least 45 seconds long' and 'self-contained (complete thought/topic)'.
  • ✓ [data] Each identified clip includes benefit-driven captions and defined start and end time boundaries derived from the transcript
    The clip identification prompt requires 'benefit-driven captions (what viewer will learn/gain)' and 'clear start and end timestamps' in the JSON output format, which is confirmed by the pinData showing startTime, endTime, and caption fields.
  • ✓ [data] Description generation stage creates YouTube descriptions with chapter timestamps formatted as MM:SS or HH:MM:SS derived from the transcript
    The 'Generate YouTube Description' prompt explicitly requires chapter timestamps in 'MM:SS or HH:MM:SS format' derived from the transcript, with format '00:00 - Introduction'. The pinData confirms this format is used.
  • ✓ [structure] Stores all generated outputs (titles, thumbnail prompts, clips, descriptions) in a persistent data table for coordination
    The 'Store in Airtable' node stores all generated outputs (SEO Title, Curiosity Title, Hybrid Title, Thumbnail Prompt, Clips, Description) in an Airtable database, which is a persistent data table.
  • ✓ [data] Transcript data with timestamps retrieved from Apify is passed as input into each of the four AI processing stages
    All four AI agent nodes receive transcript data from 'Scrape YouTube Transcript' via direct connections, and each prompt includes '{{ JSON.stringify($json.transcript) }}' passing the transcript with timestamps as input.

Generated Code (341 lines)

{
  "id": "youtube-content-repurposing",
  "name": "YouTube Content Repurposing Pipeline",
  "nodes": [
    {
      "id": "b6ec06e7-4018-4dc3-8636-e8ae49ad971b",
      "name": "Start",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.manualTrigger",
      "typeVersion": 1,
      "position": [
        240,
        400
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "a5f0d205-531d-4c0b-964c-e8715d24592a",
      "name": "Scrape YouTube Transcript",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest",
      "typeVersion": 4.4,
      "position": [
        540,
        400
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "method": "POST",
        "url": "<__PLACEHOLDER_VALUE__Apify Actor API URL (e.g., https://api.apify.com/v2/acts/YOUR_ACTOR_ID/runs)__>",
        "authentication": "genericCredentialType",
        "genericAuthType": "httpHeaderAuth",
        "sendBody": true,
        "contentType": "json",
        "specifyBody": "json",
        "jsonBody": "={{ { \"videoUrl\": $json.videoUrl } }}",
        "options": {
          "timeout": 120000
        }
      },
      "credentials": {}
    },
    {
      "id": "6521aad8-61fa-403b-86cf-45985c02da89",
      "name": "Generate Title Variations",
      "type": "@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.agent",
      "typeVersion": 3.1,
      "position": [
        840,
        200
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "promptType": "define",
        "text": "=Generate three title variations for this YouTube video based on the transcript:\n\nVideo Title: {{ $json.videoTitle }}\nTranscript: {{ JSON.stringify($json.transcript) }}\n\nCreate exactly three title variations:\n1. SEO-Focused: Optimize for search with clear keywords (40-60 characters)\n2. Curiosity-Driven: Use power words and intrigue to maximize CTR (40-60 characters)\n3. Hybrid: Balance SEO and curiosity (40-60 characters)\n\nPower words to consider: Ultimate, Secret, Proven, Essential, Revolutionary, Game-Changing\nFormat your response as JSON:\n{\n  \"seoTitle\": \"...\",\n  \"curiosityTitle\": \"...\",\n  \"hybridTitle\": \"...\"\n}",
        "options": {
          "systemMessage": "You are an expert YouTube content strategist specializing in title optimization for maximum CTR and SEO performance."
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "e6fceac0-ce4b-476e-9512-06f467ab89c0",
      "name": "OpenAI Model",
      "type": "@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.lmChatOpenAi",
      "typeVersion": 1.3,
      "position": [
        840,
        800
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "model": {
          "__rl": true,
          "mode": "list",
          "value": "gpt-5-mini"
        }
      },
      "credentials": {}
    },
    {
      "id": "fef7fa2d-5733-4a99-8a32-c144b97b8773",
      "name": "Combine All Outputs",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.aggregate",
      "typeVersion": 1,
      "position": [
        1140,
        500
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "aggregate": "aggregateAllItemData",
        "destinationFieldName": "aiOutputs",
        "include": "allFields"
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "5509df18-1161-42a5-b22d-1f491c60184e",
      "name": "Generate Thumbnail Prompts",
      "type": "@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.agent",
      "typeVersion": 3.1,
      "position": [
        840,
        400
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "promptType": "define",
        "text": "=Transform this rough visual concept into a detailed image generation prompt for a YouTube thumbnail:\n\nVideo Title: {{ $json.videoTitle }}\nTranscript: {{ JSON.stringify($json.transcript) }}\n\nCreate a detailed DALL-E/Midjourney prompt that includes:\n- Specific visual elements and composition\n- Font styles (bold, sans-serif for readability)\n- Color scheme (high contrast, vibrant)\n- Aspect ratio: 16:9 (1920x1080)\n- Text overlay suggestions\n- Lighting and mood\n\nFormat as a single detailed prompt ready for image generation.",
        "options": {
          "systemMessage": "You are an expert visual designer specializing in high-converting YouTube thumbnails and image generation prompts."
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "13349708-d450-4efa-8758-dd9d0daf9548",
      "name": "Identify Short-Form Clips",
      "type": "@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.agent",
      "typeVersion": 3.1,
      "position": [
        840,
        600
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "promptType": "define",
        "text": "=Analyze this YouTube transcript and identify self-contained clips suitable for short-form content:\n\nTranscript with timestamps: {{ JSON.stringify($json.transcript) }}\n\nRequirements:\n- Each clip must be at least 45 seconds long\n- Must be self-contained (complete thought/topic)\n- Identify clear start and end timestamps\n- Create benefit-driven captions (what viewer will learn/gain)\n- Ensure proper boundaries (no mid-sentence cuts)\n\nFormat your response as JSON array:\n[\n  {\n    \"startTime\": \"MM:SS\",\n    \"endTime\": \"MM:SS\",\n    \"duration\": \"seconds\",\n    \"caption\": \"Benefit-driven caption\",\n    \"topic\": \"Brief topic description\"\n  }\n]",
        "options": {
          "systemMessage": "You are an expert video editor specializing in identifying viral short-form content clips from long-form videos."
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "951ec0c0-d976-4e79-a81e-8b663863f50e",
      "name": "Generate YouTube Description",
      "type": "@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.agent",
      "typeVersion": 3.1,
      "position": [
        840,
        800
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "promptType": "define",
        "text": "=Create a YouTube video description with properly formatted chapter timestamps:\n\nVideo Title: {{ $json.videoTitle }}\nTranscript: {{ JSON.stringify($json.transcript) }}\n\nRequirements:\n- Write engaging description (2-3 paragraphs)\n- Add chapter timestamps in MM:SS or HH:MM:SS format\n- Derive timestamps from the transcript\n- Format: \"00:00 - Introduction\"\n- Include relevant hashtags\n- Add call-to-action\n\nFormat the complete description with chapters.",
        "options": {
          "systemMessage": "You are an expert YouTube content writer specializing in SEO-optimized descriptions and chapter formatting."
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "5d77b113-9c6a-415d-b887-6fe4838a8925",
      "name": "Store in Airtable",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.airtable",
      "typeVersion": 2.1,
      "position": [
        1440,
        500
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "resource": "record",
        "operation": "create",
        "base": {
          "__rl": true,
          "mode": "list",
          "value": ""
        },
        "table": {
          "__rl": true,
          "mode": "list",
          "value": ""
        },
        "columns": "={{ {\n  \"Video URL\": $(\"Scrape YouTube Transcript\").item.json.videoUrl,\n  \"Video Title\": $(\"Scrape YouTube Transcript\").item.json.videoTitle,\n  \"SEO Title\": JSON.parse($json.aiOutputs[0].output).seoTitle,\n  \"Curiosity Title\": JSON.parse($json.aiOutputs[0].output).curiosityTitle,\n  \"Hybrid Title\": JSON.parse($json.aiOutputs[0].output).hybridTitle,\n  \"Thumbnail Prompt\": $json.aiOutputs[1].output,\n  \"Clips\": $json.aiOutputs[2].output,\n  \"Description\": $json.aiOutputs[3].output,\n  \"Created At\": $now.toISO()\n} }}"
      },
      "credentials": {}
    }
  ],
  "connections": {

## data_extraction
patternHint: Source → Extract → Parse → Structure

Suggested nodes:
- n8n-nodes-base.extractFromFile
  displayName: Extract from File
  description: Convert binary data to JSON
  note: For multiple file types, route by file type first with IF/Switch
- n8n-nodes-base.htmlExtract
  displayName: (not found)
  description: (not found)
  note: JS-rendered content may be empty
- n8n-nodes-base.splitOut
  displayName: Split Out
  description: Turn a list inside item(s) into separate items
  note: Use before Loop Over Items for arrays
- n8n-nodes-base.splitInBatches
  displayName: Loop Over Items (Split in Batches)
  description: Split data into batches and iterate over each batch
  note: Process 200 rows at a time for memory
- n8n-nodes-base.code
  displayName: Code
  description: Run custom JavaScript or Python code
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.informationExtractor
  displayName: Information Extractor
  description: Extract information from text in a structured format
  note: For unstructured text
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.chainSummarization
  displayName: Summarization Chain
  description: Transforms text into a concise summary
  note: Context window limits may truncate

## content_generation
patternHint: Trigger → Generate (Text/Image/Video) → Deliver

Suggested nodes:
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.agent
  displayName: AI Agent
  description: Generates an action plan and executes it. Can use external tools.
  note: For text generation
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.openAi
  displayName: OpenAI
  description: Message an assistant or GPT, analyze images, generate audio, etc.
  note: Use for image/video generation. DALL-E, TTS, Sora video generation
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.lmChatGoogleGemini
  displayName: Google Gemini Chat Model
  description: Chat Model Google Gemini
  note: Imagen, video generation
- n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest
  displayName: HTTP Request
  description: Makes an HTTP request and returns the response data
  note: For APIs without dedicated nodes
- n8n-nodes-base.editImage
  displayName: Edit Image
  description: Edits an image like blur, resize or adding border and text
  note: Resize, crop, format conversion
- n8n-nodes-base.markdown
  displayName: Markdown
  description: Convert data between Markdown and HTML
  note: Convert to HTML
- n8n-nodes-base.facebookGraphApi
  displayName: Facebook Graph API
  description: Interacts with Facebook using the Graph API
- n8n-nodes-base.wait
  displayName: Wait
  description: Wait before continue with execution
  note: Video generation is async, use wait while polling for updated

## data_persistence
patternHint: Trigger → Process → Store (DataTable/Sheets)

Suggested nodes:
- n8n-nodes-base.dataTable
  displayName: Data table
  description: Permanently save data across workflow executions in a table
  note: PREFERRED - no external config needed
- n8n-nodes-base.googleSheets
  displayName: Google Sheets
  description: Read, update and write data to Google Sheets
  note: For collaboration needs; if >10k rows expected, consider DataTable instead
- n8n-nodes-base.airtable
  displayName: Airtable
  description: Read, update, write and delete data from Airtable
  note: If relationships between tables are needed
- n8n-nodes-base.postgres
  displayName: Postgres
  description: Get, add and update data in Postgres
- n8n-nodes-base.mySql
  displayName: MySQL
  description: Get, add and update data in MySQL
- n8n-nodes-base.mongoDb
  displayName: MongoDB
  description: Find, insert and update documents in MongoDB

## scraping_and_research
patternHint: Trigger → Fetch → Extract → Store

Suggested nodes:
- n8n-nodes-base.dataTable
  displayName: Data table
  description: Permanently save data across workflow executions in a table
  note: Default storage for scraped data when the user does not specify a destination. No external config needed. Always include a storage step in scraping workflows
- n8n-nodes-base.phantombuster
  displayName: Phantombuster
  description: Consume Phantombuster API
  note: Use this for social media requests: LinkedIn, Facebook, Instagram, Twitter, etc.
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.toolSerpApi
  displayName: SerpApi (Google Search)
  description: Search in Google using SerpAPI
  note: Give agent web search capability, get up-to-date information from websites.
- n8n-nodes-base.perplexity
  displayName: Perplexity
  description:  AI-powered answer engine that provides accurate, trusted, and real-time answers to any question. Generates AI responses with citations
  note: Recommended for fetching up-to-date news
- n8n-nodes-base.perplexityTool
  displayName: Perplexity Tool
  description:  AI-powered answer engine that provides accurate, trusted, and real-time answers to any question. Generates AI responses with citations
  note: Recommended for fetching up-to-date news
- n8n-nodes-base.htmlExtract
  displayName: (not found)
  description: (not found)
  note: Use to extract HTML content from http requests. Though, JS-rendered sites may return empty
- n8n-nodes-base.splitInBatches
  displayName: Loop Over Items (Split in Batches)
  description: Split data into batches and iterate over each batch
  note: Use to batch the processing of items. General recommendation: 200 rows at a time if processing is fast
- n8n-nodes-base.wait
  displayName: Wait
  description: Wait before continue with execution
  note: Use this to avoid rate limits (429 errors)
- n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest
  displayName: HTTP Request
  description: Makes an HTTP request and returns the response data
- n8n-nodes-base.httpRequestTool
  displayName: HTTP Request Tool
  description: Makes an HTTP request and returns the response data
#3 - · 5 in · 82 out · 0 thinking · 1 tool call
search_nodes OK
Input
{
  "queries": [
    "apify",
    "youtube",
    "airtable",
    "ai agent",
    "openai",
    "manual trigger",
    "set",
    "code",
    "merge"
  ]
}

- n8n-nodes-base.spotify
  Display Name: Spotify
  Version: 1
  Description: Access public song data via the Spotify API
  Discriminators:
    resource:
      - album:
          operations:
            - get
              Get an album by URI or ID
            - getNewReleases
              Get a list of new album releases
            - getTracks
              Get an album's tracks by URI or ID
            - search
              Search albums by keyword
      - artist:
          operations:
            - get
              Get an artist by URI or ID
            - getAlbums
              Get an artist's albums by URI or ID
            - getRelatedArtists
              Get an artist's related artists by URI or ID
            - getTopTracks
              Get an artist's top tracks by URI or ID
            - search
              Search artists by keyword
      - library:
          operations:
            - getLikedTracks
              Get the user's liked tracks
      - myData:
          operations:
            - getFollowingArtists
              Get your followed artists
      - player:
          operations:
            - addSongToQueue
              Add a song to your queue
            - currentlyPlaying
              Get your currently playing track
            - nextSong
              Skip to your next track
            - pause
              Pause your music
            - previousSong
              Skip to your previous song
            - recentlyPlayed
              Get your recently played tracks
            - resume
              Resume playback on the current active device
            - volume
              Set volume on the current active device
            - startMusic
              Start playing a playlist, artist, or album
      - playlist:
          operations:
            - add
              Add tracks to a playlist by track and playlist URI or ID
            - create
              Create a new playlist
            - get
              Get a playlist by URI or ID
            - getUserPlaylists
              Get a user's playlists
            - getTracks
              Get a playlist's tracks by URI or ID
            - delete
              Remove tracks from a playlist by track and playlist URI or ID
            - search
              Search playlists by keyword
      - track:
          operations:
            - get
              Get a track by its URI or ID
            - getAudioFeatures
              Get audio features for a track by URI or ID
            - search
              Search tracks by keyword

  Use get_node_types with discriminators:
    get_node_types({ nodeIds: [{ nodeId: "n8n-nodes-base.spotify", resource: "album", operation: "get" }] })

- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.vectorStoreSupabase
  Display Name: Supabase Vector Store
  Version: 1.3
  Description: Work with your data in Supabase Vector Store
  Discriminators:
    mode:
      - load: "Get Many" → use node()
        Get many ranked documents from vector store for query
      - insert: "Insert Documents" → use node()
        Insert documents into vector store
      - retrieve: "Retrieve Documents (As Vector Store for Chain)" → use vectorStore({ mode: 'retrieve' }) for subnodes.vectorStore
        Retrieve documents from vector store to be used as vector store with AI nodes
      - retrieve-as-tool: "Retrieve Documents (As Tool for AI Agent)" → use tool({ mode: 'retrieve-as-tool' }) for subnodes.tools
        Retrieve documents from vector store to be used as tool with AI nodes
      - update: "Update Documents" → use node()
        Update documents in vector store by ID

  Use get_node_types with discriminators:
    get_node_types({ nodeIds: [{ nodeId: "@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.vectorStoreSupabase", mode: "load" }] })

- n8n-nodes-base.timeSaved
  Display Name: Track Time Saved
  Version: 1
  Description: Dynamically track time saved based on the workflow’s execution path and the number of items processed
  Discriminators:
    mode:
      - once: "Once For All Items"
        Counts minutes saved once for all input items
      - perItem: "Per Item"
        Multiply minutes saved by the number of input items

  Use get_node_types with discriminators:
    get_node_types({ nodeIds: [{ nodeId: "n8n-nodes-base.timeSaved", mode: "once" }] })

---

## "airtable"
Found 5 nodes:

- n8n-nodes-base.airtable
  Display Name: Airtable
  Version: 2.1
  Description: Read, update, write and delete data from Airtable
  Discriminators:
    resource:
      - base:
          operations:
            - getMany
              List all the bases
            - getSchema
              Get the schema of the tables in a base
      - record:
          operations:
            - create
              Create a new record in a table
            - upsert
              Create a new record, or update the current one if it already exists (upsert)
            - deleteRecord
              Delete a record from a table
            - get
              Retrieve a record from a table
            - search
              Search for specific records or list all
            - update
              Update a record in a table

  Use get_node_types with discriminators:
    get_node_types({ nodeIds: [{ nodeId: "n8n-nodes-base.airtable", resource: "base", operation: "getMany" }] })

  Version: 2.1

  Discriminators:
    resource:
      - base:
          operations:
            - getMany
              List all the bases
            - getSchema
              Get the schema of the tables in a base
      - record:
          operations:
            - create
              Create a new record in a table
            - upsert
              Create a new record, or update the current one if it already exists (upsert)
            - deleteRecord
              Delete a record from a table
            - get
              Retrieve a record from a table
            - search
              Search for specific records or list all
            - update
              Update a record in a table

  Use get_node_types with discriminators:
    get_node_types({ nodeIds: [{ nodeId: "n8n-nodes-base.airtableTool", resource: "base", operation: "getMany" }] })

- n8n-nodes-base.airtableTrigger [TRIGGER]
  Display Name: Airtable Trigger
  Version: 1
  Description: Starts the workflow when Airtable events occur
  Discriminators: none (use node directly without resource/operation/mode)

- n8n-nodes-base.dataTable
  Display Name: Data table
  Version: 1.1
  Description: Permanently save data across workflow executions in a table
  Discriminators:
    resource:
      - row:
          operations:
            - deleteRows
              Delete row(s)
            - get
              Get row(s)
            - rowExists
              Match input items that are in the data table
            - rowNotExists
              Match input items that are not in the data table
            - insert
              Insert a new row
            - update
              Update row(s) matching certain fields
            - upsert
              Update row(s), or insert if there is no match
      - table:
          operations:
            - create
              Create a new data table
            - delete
              Delete a data table
            - list
              List all data tables
            - update
              Update a data table name

  Use get_node_types with discriminators:
    get_node_types({ nodeIds: [{ nodeId: "n8n-nodes-base.dataTable", resource: "row", operation: "deleteRows" }] })

## "ai agent"
Found 5 nodes:

- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.agent
  Display Name: AI Agent
  Version: 3.1
  Description: Generates an action plan and executes it. Can use external tools.
  @relatedNodes
    - n8n-nodes-base.aggregate: "Use to combine multiple items together before the agent"
    - @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.outputParserStructured: "Attach for structured output; reference fields as $json.output.fieldName for use in subsequent nodes (conditions, storing data)"
    - @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.agentTool: "For multi-agent systems using orchestrator pattern"
    - @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.memoryBufferWindow: "Required for conversational workflows - connect memory to every agent that needs to recall previous messages in the conversation"
  Discriminators: none (use node directly without resource/operation/mode)

  Display Name: AI Agent Tool

  Discriminators: none (use node directly without resource/operation/mode)

## "openai"
Found 5 nodes:

- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.openAi
  Display Name: OpenAI
  Version: 2.1
  Description: Message an assistant or GPT, analyze images, generate audio, etc.
  @builderHint For text generation, reasoning and tools, use AI Agent with OpenAI Chat Model. This OpenAI node is for specialized operations: image generation (DALL-E), audio (Whisper, TTS), and video generation (Sora).
  @relatedNodes
    - @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.agent: "Prefer for most LLM tasks"
    - @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.lmChatOpenAi: "Prefer for most LLM tasks"
  Discriminators:
    resource:
      - text:
          @builderHint For text generation, reasoning and tools, use AI Agent with OpenAI Chat Model instead of this resource.
          operations:
            - response
              Generate a model response with GPT 3, 4, 5, etc. using Responses API
            - classify
              Check whether content complies with usage policies
      - image:
          operations:
            - analyze
              Take in images and answer questions about them
            - generate
              Creates an image from a text prompt
            - edit
              Edit an image
      - audio:
          operations:
            - generate
              Creates audio from a text prompt
            - transcribe
              Transcribes audio into text
            - translate
              Translates audio into text in English
      - file:
          operations:
            - deleteFile
              Delete a file from the server
            - list
              Returns a list of files that belong to the user's organization
            - upload
              Upload a file that can be used across various endpoints
      - conversation:
          operations:
            - create
              Create a conversation
            - get
              Get a conversation
            - remove
              Remove a conversation
            - update
              Update a conversation
      - video:
          operations:
            - generate
              Creates a video from a text prompt

  Use get_node_types with discriminators:
    get_node_types({ nodeIds: [{ nodeId: "@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.openAi", resource: "text", operation: "response" }] })

- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.lmChatOpenAi
  Display Name: OpenAI Chat Model
  Version: 1.3
  Description: For advanced usage with an AI chain
  Discriminators: none (use node directly without resource/operation/mode)

- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.lmChatAzureOpenAi
  Display Name: Azure OpenAI Chat Model
  Version: 1

- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.embeddingsOpenAi

- n8n-nodes-base.set

    mode:
      - manual: "Manual Mapping"
        Edit item fields one by one
      - raw: "JSON"

    get_node_types({ nodeIds: [{ nodeId: "n8n-nodes-base.set", mode: "manual" }] })

- n8n-nodes-base.seaTable
  Display Name: SeaTable
  Version: 2
  Description: Consume the SeaTable API
  Discriminators:
    resource:
      - row:
          operations:
            - create
              Create a new row
            - remove
              Delete a row
            - get
              Get the content of a row
            - list
              Get many rows from a table or a table view
            - lock
              Lock a row to prevent further changes
            - search
              Search one or multiple rows
            - unlock
              Remove the lock from a row
            - update
              Update the content of a row
      - base:
          operations:
            - snapshot
              Create a snapshot of the base
            - metadata
              Get the complete metadata of the base
            - collaborator
              Get the username from the email or name of a collaborator
      - link:
          operations:
            - add
              Create a link between two rows in a link column
            - list
              List all links of a specific row
            - remove
              Remove a link between two rows from a link column
      - asset:
          operations:
            - getPublicURL
              Get the public URL from asset path
            - upload
              Add a file/image to an existing row

  Use get_node_types with discriminators:
    get_node_types({ nodeIds: [{ nodeId: "n8n-nodes-base.seaTable", resource: "row", operation: "create" }] })

  Display Name: Segment Tool
  Version: 1
  Description: Consume Segment API
  Discriminators:
    resource:
      - group:
          Group lets you associate an identified user with a group
          operations:
            - add
              Add a user to a group
      - identify:
          Identify lets you tie a user to their actions
          operations:
            - create
              Create an identity
      - track:
          Track lets you record events
          operations:
            - event
              Record the actions your users perform. Every action triggers an event, which can also have associated properties.
            - page
              Record page views on your website, along with optional extra information about the page being viewed

  Use get_node_types with discriminators:
    get_node_types({ nodeIds: [{ nodeId: "n8n-nodes-base.segmentTool", resource: "group", operation: "add" }] })

## "code"
Found 5 nodes:

- n8n-nodes-base.code
  Display Name: Code
  Version: 2
  Description: Run custom JavaScript or Python code
  @builderHint Use Code node as a LAST RESORT — it runs in a sandboxed environment and is slower than native nodes. Code node is ONLY appropriate for complex multi-step algorithms that cannot be expressed in single expressions, or operations requiring complex data structures.
  @relatedNodes
    - n8n-nodes-base.set: "Use this instead for data manipulation: add/modify/rename fields, set values, map data"
    - n8n-nodes-base.filter: "Use this instead for filtering items by condition"
    - n8n-nodes-base.if: "Use this instead for routing by condition"
    - n8n-nodes-base.switch: "Use this instead for multi-way routing by condition"
    - n8n-nodes-base.splitOut: "Use this instead for splitting arrays into separate items"
    - n8n-nodes-base.aggregate: "Use this instead for combining multiple items into one"
    - n8n-nodes-base.summarize: "Use this instead for summarizing or pivoting data"
    - n8n-nodes-base.removeDuplicates: "Use this instead for removing duplicates"
    - n8n-nodes-base.limit: "Use this instead to reduce the number of items returned"
    - n8n-nodes-base.merge: "Use this instead for merging data from multiple branches"
    - n8n-nodes-base.dateTime: "Use this instead for date time operations"
    - n8n-nodes-base.html: "Use this instead for creating html pages"
  Discriminators:
    mode:
      - runOnceForAllItems: "Run Once for All Items"
        Run this code only once, no matter how many input items there are
      - runOnceForEachItem: "Run Once for Each Item"
        Run this code as many times as there are input items

  Use get_node_types with discriminators:
    get_node_types({ nodeIds: [{ nodeId: "n8n-nodes-base.code", mode: "runOnceForAllItems" }] })

- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.toolCode
  Display Name: Code Tool
  Version: 1.3
  Description: Write a tool in JS or Python
  Discriminators: none (use node directly without resource/operation/mode)

- n8n-nodes-base.aiTransform
  Display Name: AI Transform
  Version: 1
  Description: Modify data based on instructions written in plain english
  Discriminators: none (use node directly without resource/operation/mode)

- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.lmCohere
  Display Name: Cohere Model
  Version: 1
  Description: Language Model Cohere
  Discriminators: none (use node directly without resource/operation/mode)

- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.lmChatCohere
  Display Name: Cohere Chat Model
  Version: 1
  Description: For advanced usage with an AI chain
  Discriminators: none (use node directly without resource/operation/mode)

## "merge"
Found 5 nodes:

- n8n-nodes-base.merge
  Display Name: Merge
  Version: 3.2
  Description: Merges data of multiple streams once data from both is available
  Discriminators:
    mode:
      - append: "Append"
        Output items of each input, one after the other
        @builderHint Append items from multiple branches into a single list sequentially. Waits for all running branches. Supports any number of inputs. @example input1: [{ x }] [{ y }] input2: [{ z }]. Output: [{ x }, { y }, { z }]. Next node will execute 3 times with each item. Set executeOnce on next node to execute once.
      - combine: "Combine"
        Merge matching items together
        @builderHint Combines items from 2 branches. Waits for both to have input data. @example **combine by position** input1: [{ x }, { y }] input2: [{ z }] output: [{ x, y }, { x: undefined, y: undefined, z }] @example **combine by key** input1: [{ id: 1, x }, { id: 2, y }] input2: [{ id: 1, z }] output: [{ id: 1, x, z }, { id: 2, y }]
      - combineBySql: "SQL Query"
        Write a query to do the merge
        @builderHint Need to combine more than 2 branches? Use SQL Query for advanced operations. Waits for all inputs. @example Results depend on query - can filter, join, aggregate
      - chooseBranch: "Choose Branch"
        Output data from a specific branch, without modifying it
        @builderHint Do you need to select data from only ONE specific input and discard the others? Use Choose Branch after conditional nodes to pick which path to continue. Waits for all inputs. @example 3 items from Input A + 2 items from Input B, choose Input A → 3 items

  Use get_node_types with discriminators:
    get_node_types({ nodeIds: [{ nodeId: "n8n-nodes-base.merge", mode: "append" }] })

- n8n-nodes-base.mailerLiteTrigger [TRIGGER]

  Version: 2
  Description: Starts the workflow when MailerLite events occur
  Discriminators: none (use node directly without resource/operation/mode)

  Display Name: MCP Server Trigger

  Description: Expose n8n tools as an MCP Server endpoint
  Discriminators: none (use node directly without resource/operation/mode)

  Display Name: Emelia Trigger

  Description: Handle Emelia campaign activity events via webhooks

## "http request"
Found 2 nodes:

- n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest
  Display Name: HTTP Request
  Version: 4.4
  Description: Makes an HTTP request and returns the response data
  @builderHint Prefer dedicated integration nodes over HTTP Request — n8n has 400+ dedicated nodes (e.g. Gmail, Slack, Google Sheets, Notion, OpenAI, HubSpot, Jira, etc.) with built-in authentication, pre-configured parameters, better error handling, and easier maintenance. Only use HTTP Request when no dedicated node exists for the service, the user explicitly requests it, accessing a custom/internal API, or the dedicated node does not support the specific operation needed.
  Discriminators: none (use node directly without resource/operation/mode)

- n8n-nodes-base.httpRequestTool
  Display Name: HTTP Request Tool
  Version: 4.4
  Description: Makes an HTTP request and returns the response data
  @builderHint Prefer dedicated integration nodes over HTTP Request — n8n has 400+ dedicated nodes (e.g. Gmail, Slack, Google Sheets, Notion, OpenAI, HubSpot, Jira, etc.) with built-in authentication, pre-configured parameters, better error handling, and easier maintenance. Only use HTTP Request when no dedicated node exists for the service, the user explicitly requests it, accessing a custom/internal API, or the dedicated node does not support the specific operation needed.

```typescript
/**
 * Manual Trigger Node - Version 1
 * Runs the flow on clicking a button in n8n
 */


export interface ManualTriggerV1Params {
}

interface ManualTriggerV1NodeBase {
  type: 'n8n-nodes-base.manualTrigger';
  version: 1;
  isTrigger: true;
}

export type ManualTriggerV1ParamsNode = ManualTriggerV1NodeBase & {
  config: NodeConfig<ManualTriggerV1Params>;
};

export type ManualTriggerV1Node = ManualTriggerV1ParamsNode;
```
