      ],
      "parameters": {
        "promptType": "define",
        "text": "={{ $json.chatInput }}",
        "options": {
          "systemMessage": "You are a helpful project management assistant. You can help users manage their project tasks using the following tools: create-task, list-tasks, update-task-status, add-comment, and get-project-summary. When users ask to create tasks, list tasks, update status, add comments, or get project summaries, use the appropriate tool. Always confirm actions and provide clear feedback about what was done."
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "f664c0da-85fe-42fb-987c-56e2ad381fd8",

      "type": "@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.lmChatOpenAi",

          "mode": "list",

      },
      "credentials": {}

    {
      "id": "013c4472-ce06-423f-9afc-ab3a0a7ff4da",
      "name": "create-task",
      "type": "@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.toolCode",
      "typeVersion": 1.3,
      "position": [
        700,
        300
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "description": "Creates a new task with title, description, priority (high/medium/low), and assignee. Returns a task ID.",
        "language": "javaScript",
        "specifyInputSchema": true,
        "schemaType": "fromJson",
        "jsonSchemaExample": {
          "title": "Implement login feature",
          "description": "Add OAuth2 authentication to the application",
          "priority": "high",
          "assignee": "john@example.com"
        },
        "jsCode": "const taskId = \"TASK-\" + Math.random().toString(36).substr(2, 9).toUpperCase();\nconst task = {\n  id: taskId,\n  title: query.title,\n  description: query.description,\n  priority: query.priority,\n  assignee: query.assignee,\n  status: \"open\",\n  createdAt: new Date().toISOString()\n};\nreturn `Task created successfully with ID: ${taskId}. Title: \"${task.title}\", Priority: ${task.priority}, Assigned to: ${task.assignee}`;"
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "3255a3c1-a683-4c43-98f8-c2ff17eb23e2",
      "name": "list-tasks",
      "type": "@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.toolCode",
      "typeVersion": 1.3,
      "position": [
        700,
        400
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "description": "Lists all tasks for a project. Optionally filter by status (open/in-progress/done). Returns an array of tasks.",
        "language": "javaScript",
        "specifyInputSchema": true,
        "schemaType": "fromJson",
        "jsonSchemaExample": {
          "projectId": "PROJ-123",
          "status": "open"
        },
        "jsCode": "const mockTasks = [\n  { id: \"TASK-001\", title: \"Setup database\", status: \"done\", priority: \"high\", assignee: \"alice@example.com\" },\n  { id: \"TASK-002\", title: \"Design UI mockups\", status: \"in-progress\", priority: \"medium\", assignee: \"bob@example.com\" },\n  { id: \"TASK-003\", title: \"Write API documentation\", status: \"open\", priority: \"low\", assignee: \"charlie@example.com\" },\n  { id: \"TASK-004\", title: \"Implement authentication\", status: \"open\", priority: \"high\", assignee: \"alice@example.com\" }\n];\nlet filteredTasks = mockTasks;\nif (query.status) {\n  filteredTasks = mockTasks.filter(task => task.status === query.status);\n}\nreturn `Found ${filteredTasks.length} tasks for project ${query.projectId}${query.status ? \" with status \" + query.status : \"\"}:\\n` + filteredTasks.map(t => `- ${t.id}: ${t.title} [${t.status}] (${t.priority}) - ${t.assignee}`).join(\"\\n\");"
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "eef54bce-169c-4de4-a516-274112f1378c",
      "name": "update-task-status",
      "type": "@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.toolCode",
      "typeVersion": 1.3,
      "position": [
        700,
        500
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "description": "Updates the status of a task. Accepts task ID and new status (open/in-progress/done). Returns the updated task.",
        "language": "javaScript",
        "specifyInputSchema": true,
        "schemaType": "fromJson",
        "jsonSchemaExample": {
          "taskId": "TASK-001",
          "newStatus": "in-progress"
        },
        "jsCode": "const updatedTask = {\n  id: query.taskId,\n  status: query.newStatus,\n  updatedAt: new Date().toISOString()\n};\nreturn `Task ${query.taskId} status updated to \"${query.newStatus}\" successfully. Updated at: ${updatedTask.updatedAt}`;"
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "55323ce2-b2cf-4ab8-95b2-949c7838eccf",
      "name": "add-comment",
      "type": "@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.toolCode",
      "typeVersion": 1.3,
      "position": [
        700,
        600
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "description": "Adds a comment to a task. Accepts task ID and comment text. Returns a comment ID.",
        "language": "javaScript",
        "specifyInputSchema": true,
        "schemaType": "fromJson",
        "jsonSchemaExample": {
          "taskId": "TASK-001",
          "commentText": "This is a comment on the task"
        },
        "jsCode": "const commentId = \"COMMENT-\" + Math.random().toString(36).substr(2, 9).toUpperCase();\nconst comment = {\n  id: commentId,\n  taskId: query.taskId,\n  text: query.commentText,\n  createdAt: new Date().toISOString()\n};\nreturn `Comment added successfully with ID: ${commentId} to task ${query.taskId}. Comment: \"${query.commentText}\"`;"
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "2babab81-01cd-416c-939e-a743eea78ba1",
      "name": "get-project-summary",
      "type": "@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.toolCode",
      "typeVersion": 1.3,
      "position": [
        700,
        700
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "description": "Gets a summary of a project including total tasks, tasks by status, and overdue count. Accepts project ID.",
        "language": "javaScript",
        "specifyInputSchema": true,
        "schemaType": "fromJson",
        "jsonSchemaExample": {
          "projectId": "PROJ-123"
        },
        "jsCode": "const summary = {\n  projectId: query.projectId,\n  totalTasks: 15,\n  tasksByStatus: {\n    open: 6,\n    \"in-progress\": 5,\n    done: 4\n  },\n  overdueCount: 2,\n  generatedAt: new Date().toISOString()\n};\nreturn `Project ${query.projectId} Summary:\\n` +\n  `Total Tasks: ${summary.totalTasks}\\n` +\n  `Open: ${summary.tasksByStatus.open}\\n` +\n  `In Progress: ${summary.tasksByStatus[\"in-progress\"]}\\n` +\n  `Done: ${summary.tasksByStatus.done}\\n` +\n  `Overdue: ${summary.overdueCount}`;"
      }
    }
  ],

COMPLEX Create a workflow suite for a contacts management app: (1) A GET endpoint at /co... auth data-table html PASS 13/13 (100%) 6 get_suggested_nodes search_nodes get_node_types str_replace_based_edit_tool batch_str_replace 1.8m 26 11,025 598

Prompt

Create a workflow suite for a contacts management app: (1) A GET endpoint at /contacts that reads all rows from a 'contacts' data table and returns an HTML page listing each contact's name, email, phone, and company in a table. (2) A GET endpoint at /contacts/new that serves an HTML page with a form containing fields for name, email, phone, and company. (3) A POST endpoint at /contacts protected by basic auth that receives the form data, validates all four fields are present, stores the contact in the 'contacts' data table, and returns a success HTML page. (4) A POST endpoint at /contacts/delete protected by basic auth that receives a contactId, deletes the matching row from the data table, and returns a confirmation.

Workflow Preview

Checklist (13/13)

  • ✓ [structure] Has a GET endpoint at /contacts
    There is a webhook node named 'GET /contacts' with httpMethod 'GET' and path 'contacts'
  • ✓ [structure] Has a GET endpoint at /contacts/new
    There is a webhook node named 'GET /contacts/new' with httpMethod 'GET' and path 'contacts/new'
  • ✓ [structure] Has a POST endpoint at /contacts protected by basic auth
    There is a webhook node named 'POST /contacts' with httpMethod 'POST', path 'contacts', and authentication 'basicAuth'
  • ✓ [structure] Has a POST endpoint at /contacts/delete protected by basic auth
    There is a webhook node named 'POST /contacts/delete' with httpMethod 'POST', path 'contacts/delete', and authentication 'basicAuth'
  • ✓ [data] GET /contacts reads all rows from the 'contacts' data table
    The 'Fetch All Contacts' node uses n8n-nodes-base.dataTable with resource 'row', operation 'get', and returnAll true, reading from the contacts data table
  • ✓ [data] GET /contacts returns an HTML page listing each contact's name, email, phone, and company in a table
    The 'Generate Contacts List HTML' node produces an HTML table with columns for Name, Email, Phone, and Company, populated from the fetched contacts data
  • ✓ [data] GET /contacts/new returns an HTML page with a form containing fields for name, email, phone, and company
    The 'Generate New Contact Form' node produces an HTML form with input fields for name, email, phone, and company
  • ✓ [structure] POST /contacts branches based on whether all four fields (name, email, phone, company) are present in the submitted form data
    The 'Validate All Fields Present' IF node checks that $json.body.name, $json.body.email, $json.body.phone, and $json.body.company are all notEmpty, branching based on all four fields
  • ✓ [data] POST /contacts stores the submitted contact (name, email, phone, company) into the 'contacts' data table when validation passes
    The 'Insert Contact' node uses n8n-nodes-base.dataTable with operation 'insert', mapping name, email, phone, and company from the request body into the contacts data table
  • ✓ [data] POST /contacts returns a success HTML page after successfully storing the contact
    After inserting, 'Generate Success HTML' creates a success HTML page and 'Respond Success' returns it as the response
  • ✓ [data] POST /contacts/delete receives a contactId field from the request
    The 'Delete Contact Row' node uses $json.body.contactId as the filter value, indicating it receives a contactId field from the request
  • ✓ [data] POST /contacts/delete deletes the row matching the provided contactId from the 'contacts' data table
    The 'Delete Contact Row' node uses dataTable operation 'deleteRows' filtering by id eq $json.body.contactId, deleting the matching row from the contacts table
  • ✓ [data] POST /contacts/delete returns a confirmation HTML page or response after deletion
    After deletion, 'Generate Delete Confirmation' creates a confirmation HTML page and 'Respond Delete Confirmation' returns it as the response

Generated Code (598 lines)

{
  "id": "contacts-management-suite",
  "name": "Contacts Management Suite",
  "nodes": [
    {
      "id": "26991937-b6ff-410f-a333-dae79fafe961",
      "name": "GET /contacts",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.webhook",
      "typeVersion": 2.1,
      "position": [
        240,
        300
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "httpMethod": "GET",
        "path": "contacts",
        "responseMode": "responseNode"
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "4ec72218-8587-4f16-a3fa-1c0f0d68407c",
      "name": "Fetch All Contacts",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.dataTable",
      "typeVersion": 1.1,
      "position": [
        540,
        300
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "resource": "row",
        "operation": "get",
        "dataTableId": {
          "__rl": true,
          "mode": "list",
          "value": ""
        },
        "returnAll": true
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "19e4ae5b-3ed2-4c57-85b7-a2a93ff1ff43",
      "name": "Generate Contacts List HTML",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.html",
      "typeVersion": 1.2,
      "position": [
        840,
        300
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "operation": "generateHtmlTemplate",
        "html": "=<!DOCTYPE html>\n<html>\n<head>\n  <title>Contacts</title>\n  <style>\n    body { font-family: Arial, sans-serif; margin: 40px; }\n    h1 { color: #333; }\n    table { border-collapse: collapse; width: 100%; margin-top: 20px; }\n    th, td { border: 1px solid #ddd; padding: 12px; text-align: left; }\n    th { background-color: #4CAF50; color: white; }\n    tr:nth-child(even) { background-color: #f2f2f2; }\n    .actions { margin-top: 20px; }\n    a { color: #4CAF50; text-decoration: none; }\n  </style>\n</head>\n<body>\n  <h1>Contacts</h1>\n  <div class=\"actions\">\n    <a href=\"/webhook/contacts/new\">Add New Contact</a>\n  </div>\n  <table>\n    <thead>\n      <tr>\n        <th>Name</th>\n        <th>Email</th>\n        <th>Phone</th>\n        <th>Company</th>\n      </tr>\n    </thead>\n    <tbody>\n      {{ $(\"Fetch All Contacts\").all().map(item => `\n        <tr>\n          <td>${item.json.name}</td>\n          <td>${item.json.email}</td>\n          <td>${item.json.phone}</td>\n          <td>${item.json.company}</td>\n        </tr>\n      `).join(\"\") }}\n    </tbody>\n  </table>\n</body>\n</html>"
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "a5968283-b0f2-46f6-83de-6966a862fb80",
      "name": "Respond Contacts List",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.respondToWebhook",
      "typeVersion": 1.5,
      "position": [
        1140,
        300
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "respondWith": "text",

      "id": "941d90c8-cea2-41f2-8b50-75471520a669",
      "name": "GET /contacts/new",

        240,

      }
    },
    {
      "id": "74ac07a4-35f6-4fb2-ad82-e3b07b15b298",
      "name": "Generate New Contact Form",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.html",
      "typeVersion": 1.2,
      "position": [
        540,
        600
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "operation": "generateHtmlTemplate",
        "html": "=<!DOCTYPE html>\n<html>\n<head>\n  <title>New Contact</title>\n  <style>\n    body { font-family: Arial, sans-serif; margin: 40px; }\n    h1 { color: #333; }\n    form { max-width: 500px; }\n    label { display: block; margin-top: 15px; font-weight: bold; }\n    input { width: 100%; padding: 8px; margin-top: 5px; border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 4px; }\n    button { margin-top: 20px; padding: 10px 20px; background-color: #4CAF50; color: white; border: none; border-radius: 4px; cursor: pointer; }\n    button:hover { background-color: #45a049; }\n    .back { margin-top: 20px; }\n    a { color: #4CAF50; text-decoration: none; }\n  </style>\n</head>\n<body>\n  <h1>Add New Contact</h1>\n  <form action=\"/webhook/contacts\" method=\"POST\">\n    <label for=\"name\">Name:</label>\n    <input type=\"text\" id=\"name\" name=\"name\" required>\n    \n    <label for=\"email\">Email:</label>\n    <input type=\"email\" id=\"email\" name=\"email\" required>\n    \n    <label for=\"phone\">Phone:</label>\n    <input type=\"tel\" id=\"phone\" name=\"phone\" required>\n    \n    <label for=\"company\">Company:</label>\n    <input type=\"text\" id=\"company\" name=\"company\" required>\n    \n    <button type=\"submit\">Add Contact</button>\n  </form>\n  <div class=\"back\">\n    <a href=\"/webhook/contacts\">Back to Contacts</a>\n  </div>\n</body>\n</html>"
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "efbc7cdc-2201-4a33-9ae4-864209c4f0fe",
      "name": "Respond New Contact Form",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.respondToWebhook",

    {
      "id": "d7993f2f-70b9-4513-ba63-8613ec9a6d94",
      "name": "Generate Success HTML",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.html",
      "typeVersion": 1.2,
      "position": [
        1140,
        800
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "operation": "generateHtmlTemplate",
        "html": "=<!DOCTYPE html>\n<html>\n<head>\n  <title>Contact Added</title>\n  <style>\n    body { font-family: Arial, sans-serif; margin: 40px; }\n    .success { color: #4CAF50; }\n    .info { background-color: #f0f0f0; padding: 15px; border-radius: 4px; margin-top: 20px; }\n    a { color: #4CAF50; text-decoration: none; }\n  </style>\n</head>\n<body>\n  <h1 class=\"success\">Contact Added Successfully!</h1>\n  <div class=\"info\">\n    <p><strong>Name:</strong> {{ $json.name }}</p>\n    <p><strong>Email:</strong> {{ $json.email }}</p>\n    <p><strong>Phone:</strong> {{ $json.phone }}</p>\n    <p><strong>Company:</strong> {{ $json.company }}</p>\n  </div>\n  <p><a href=\"/webhook/contacts\">View All Contacts</a></p>\n</body>\n</html>"
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "2500739a-02fb-4eae-bc33-926c69d59901",
      "name": "Respond Success",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.respondToWebhook",

Output
## data_persistence
patternHint: Trigger → Process → Store (DataTable/Sheets)

Suggested nodes:
- n8n-nodes-base.dataTable
  displayName: Data table
  description: Permanently save data across workflow executions in a table
  note: PREFERRED - no external config needed
- n8n-nodes-base.googleSheets
  displayName: Google Sheets
  description: Read, update and write data to Google Sheets
  note: For collaboration needs; if >10k rows expected, consider DataTable instead
- n8n-nodes-base.airtable
  displayName: Airtable
  description: Read, update, write and delete data from Airtable
  note: If relationships between tables are needed
- n8n-nodes-base.postgres
  displayName: Postgres
  description: Get, add and update data in Postgres
- n8n-nodes-base.mySql
  displayName: MySQL
  description: Get, add and update data in MySQL
- n8n-nodes-base.mongoDb
  displayName: MongoDB
  description: Find, insert and update documents in MongoDB

## form_input
patternHint: Form Trigger → Validate → Store → Respond

Suggested nodes:
- n8n-nodes-base.formTrigger
  displayName: n8n Form Trigger
  description: Generate webforms in n8n and pass their responses to the workflow
  note: ALWAYS store raw data to persistent storage
- n8n-nodes-base.form
  displayName: n8n Form
  description: Generate webforms in n8n and pass their responses to the workflow
  note: Each node is one page/step
- n8n-nodes-base.dataTable
  displayName: Data table
  description: Permanently save data across workflow executions in a table
  note: PREFERRED for form data storage
- n8n-nodes-base.googleSheets
  displayName: Google Sheets
  description: Read, update and write data to Google Sheets
- n8n-nodes-base.airtable
  displayName: Airtable
  description: Read, update, write and delete data from Airtable

## "webhook"
Found 5 nodes:

- n8n-nodes-base.webhook [TRIGGER]
  Display Name: Webhook
  Version: 2.1
  Description: Starts the workflow when a webhook is called
  Discriminators: none (use node directly without resource/operation/mode)

- n8n-nodes-base.respondToWebhook [TRIGGER]
  Display Name: Respond to Webhook
  Version: 1.5
  Description: Returns data for Webhook
  @builderHint Only works with webhook node (n8n-nodes-base.webhook) with responseMode set to "responseNode"
  @relatedNodes
    - n8n-nodes-base.webhook: "Required trigger - set responseMode to "responseNode""
  Discriminators: none (use node directly without resource/operation/mode)

- n8n-nodes-base.flowTrigger [TRIGGER]
  Display Name: Flow Trigger
  Version: 1
  Description: Handle Flow events via webhooks
  Discriminators:
    resource:
      - list:
          operations:
      - task:
          operations:

  Use get_node_types with discriminators:
    get_node_types({ nodeIds: [{ nodeId: "n8n-nodes-base.flowTrigger", resource: "list", operation: "get" }] })

- n8n-nodes-base.taigaTrigger [TRIGGER]
  Display Name: Taiga Trigger
  Version: 1
  Description: Handle Taiga events via webhook
  Discriminators: none (use node directly without resource/operation/mode)

- n8n-nodes-base.wiseTrigger [TRIGGER]
  Display Name: Wise Trigger
  Version: 1
  Description: Handle Wise events via webhooks
  Discriminators: none (use node directly without resource/operation/mode)

## "data table"
Found 5 nodes:

- n8n-nodes-base.dataTable
  Display Name: Data table
  Version: 1.1
  Description: Permanently save data across workflow executions in a table
  Discriminators:
    resource:
      - row:
          operations:
            - deleteRows
              Delete row(s)
            - get
              Get row(s)
            - rowExists
              Match input items that are in the data table
            - rowNotExists
              Match input items that are not in the data table
            - insert
              Insert a new row
            - update
              Update row(s) matching certain fields
            - upsert
              Update row(s), or insert if there is no match
      - table:
          operations:
            - create
              Create a new data table
            - delete
              Delete a data table
            - list
              List all data tables
            - update
              Update a data table name

  Use get_node_types with discriminators:
    get_node_types({ nodeIds: [{ nodeId: "n8n-nodes-base.dataTable", resource: "row", operation: "deleteRows" }] })

            - rowNotExists

## "set"
Found 5 nodes:

- n8n-nodes-base.set
  Display Name: Edit Fields (Set)
  Version: 3.4
  Description: Modify, add, or remove item fields
  Discriminators:
    mode:
      - manual: "Manual Mapping"
        Edit item fields one by one
      - raw: "JSON"
        Customize item output with JSON

  Use get_node_types with discriminators:
    get_node_types({ nodeIds: [{ nodeId: "n8n-nodes-base.set", mode: "manual" }] })

- n8n-nodes-base.seaTable
  Display Name: SeaTable
  Version: 2
  Description: Consume the SeaTable API
  Discriminators:
    resource:
      - row:
          operations:
            - create
              Create a new row
            - remove
              Delete a row
            - get
              Get the content of a row
            - list
              Get many rows from a table or a table view
            - lock
              Lock a row to prevent further changes
            - search
              Search one or multiple rows
            - unlock
              Remove the lock from a row
            - update
              Update the content of a row
      - base:
          operations:
            - snapshot
              Create a snapshot of the base
            - metadata
              Get the complete metadata of the base
            - collaborator
              Get the username from the email or name of a collaborator
      - link:
          operations:
            - add
              Create a link between two rows in a link column
            - list
              List all links of a specific row
            - remove
              Remove a link between two rows from a link column
      - asset:
          operations:
            - getPublicURL
              Get the public URL from asset path
            - upload
              Add a file/image to an existing row

  Use get_node_types with discriminators:
    get_node_types({ nodeIds: [{ nodeId: "n8n-nodes-base.seaTable", resource: "row", operation: "create" }] })

  Version: 1
  Description: Consume Sendy API

      - campaign:
          operations:
            - create
              Create a campaign
      - subscriber:
          operations:
            - add
              Add a subscriber to a list
            - count
              Count subscribers
            - delete
              Delete a subscriber from a list
            - remove
              Unsubscribe user from a list
            - status
              Get the status of subscriber

  Display Name: Segment Tool

      - identify:
          Identify lets you tie a user to their actions
          operations:
            - create
              Create an identity
      - track:
          Track lets you record events
          operations:
            - event
              Record the actions your users perform. Every action triggers an event, which can also have associated properties.
            - page
              Record page views on your website, along with optional extra information about the page being viewed

  Discriminators:

      - csv
        Transform input data into a CSV file
      - html
        Transform input data into a table in an HTML file
      - iCal
        Converts each input item to an ICS event file
      - toJson
        Transform input data into a single or multiple JSON files
      - ods
        Transform input data into an ODS file
      - rtf
        Transform input data into a table in an RTF file
      - toText
        Transform input data string into a file
      - xls
        Transform input data into an Excel file
      - xlsx
        Transform input data into an Excel file
      - toBinary
        Convert a base64-encoded string into its original file format

      - fromJson
        Transform a JSON file into output items
      - ods
        Transform an ODS file into output items

        Transform a table in an RTF file into output items

      - xml

        Transform an Excel file into output items
      - xlsx

      - binaryToPropery

## "http request"
Found 2 nodes:

- n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest
  Display Name: HTTP Request
  Version: 4.4
  Description: Makes an HTTP request and returns the response data
  @builderHint Prefer dedicated integration nodes over HTTP Request — n8n has 400+ dedicated nodes (e.g. Gmail, Slack, Google Sheets, Notion, OpenAI, HubSpot, Jira, etc.) with built-in authentication, pre-configured parameters, better error handling, and easier maintenance. Only use HTTP Request when no dedicated node exists for the service, the user explicitly requests it, accessing a custom/internal API, or the dedicated node does not support the specific operation needed.
  Discriminators: none (use node directly without resource/operation/mode)

- n8n-nodes-base.httpRequestTool
  Display Name: HTTP Request Tool
  Version: 4.4
  Description: Makes an HTTP request and returns the response data
  @builderHint Prefer dedicated integration nodes over HTTP Request — n8n has 400+ dedicated nodes (e.g. Gmail, Slack, Google Sheets, Notion, OpenAI, HubSpot, Jira, etc.) with built-in authentication, pre-configured parameters, better error handling, and easier maintenance. Only use HTTP Request when no dedicated node exists for the service, the user explicitly requests it, accessing a custom/internal API, or the dedicated node does not support the specific operation needed.
  Discriminators: none (use node directly without resource/operation/mode)

# TypeScript Type Definitions

## n8n-nodes-base.webhook (v21)

```typescript
/**
 * Webhook Node - Version 2.1
 * Starts the workflow when a webhook is called
 */


export interface WebhookV21Params {
/**
 * Whether to allow the webhook to listen for multiple HTTP methods
 * @default false
 */
    multipleMethods?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
/**
 * The HTTP method to listen to
 * @displayOptions.show { multipleMethods: [false, true] }
 * @default GET
 */
    httpMethod?: 'DELETE' | 'GET' | 'HEAD' | 'PATCH' | 'POST' | 'PUT' | Expression<string>;
/**
 * The path to listen to, dynamic values could be specified by using ':', e.g. 'your-path/:dynamic-value'. If dynamic values are set 'webhookId' would be prepended to path.
 * @builderHint The webhook path that triggers this workflow
 */
    path?: string | Expression<string>;
/**
 * The way to authenticate
 * @default none
 */
    authentication?: 'basicAuth' | 'headerAuth' | 'jwtAuth' | 'none' | Expression<string>;
/**
 * When and how to respond to the webhook
 * @builderHint Use 'responseNode' to respond via a 'Respond to Webhook' node later in the workflow
 * @default onReceived
 */
    responseMode?: 'onReceived' | 'lastNode' | 'responseNode' | 'streaming' | Expression<string>;
/**
 * What data should be returned. If it should return all items as an array or only the first item as object.
 * @displayOptions.show { responseMode: ["lastNode"] }
 * @default firstEntryJson
 */
    responseData?: 'allEntries' | 'firstEntryJson' | 'firstEntryBinary' | 'noData' | Expression<string>;
/**
 * Name of the binary property to return
 * @displayOptions.show { responseData: ["firstEntryBinary"] }
 * @default data
 */
    responseBinaryPropertyName?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
  options?: {
    /** Comma-separated list of URLs allowed for cross-origin non-preflight requests. Use * (default) to allow all origins.
     * @default *
     */
    allowedOrigins?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
    /** Whether the webhook will receive binary data
     * @displayOptions.show { /httpMethod: ["PATCH", "PUT", "POST"] }
     * @default false
     */
    binaryData?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
    /** The name of the output field to put any binary file data in. Only relevant if binary data is received.
     * @default data
     */
    binaryPropertyName?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
    /** Whether to ignore requests from bots like link previewers and web crawlers
     * @default false
     */
    ignoreBots?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
    /** Comma-separated list of allowed IP addresses or CIDR ranges. Leave empty to allow all IPs.
     */
    ipWhitelist?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
    /** Whether to send any body in the response
     * @displayOptions.show { /responseMode: ["onReceived"] }
     * @displayOptions.hide { rawBody: [true] }
     * @default false
     */
    noResponseBody?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
    /** Name of the property to return the data of instead of the whole JSON
     * @displayOptions.show { /responseData: ["firstEntryJson"], /responseMode: ["lastNode"] }
     * @default data
     */
    responsePropertyName?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
    /** If the data gets received via "Form-Data Multipart" it will be the prefix and a number starting with 0 will be attached to it
     * @hint The name of the output binary field to put the file in
     * @displayOptions.show { binaryData: [true] }
     * @default data
     */
    binaryPropertyName?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
    /** Raw body (binary)
     * @displayOptions.hide { binaryData: [true], noResponseBody: [true] }
     * @default false
     */
    rawBody?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
    /** Whether to return the raw body
     * @displayOptions.hide { noResponseBody: [true] }
     * @default false
     */
    rawBody?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
    /** Response Code
     * @displayOptions.hide { /responseMode: ["responseNode"] }
     * @default {"values":{"responseCode":200}}
     */
    responseCode?: {
        /** Values
     */
    values?: {
      /** The HTTP response code to return
       * @default 200
       */
      responseCode?: 200 | 201 | 204 | 301 | 302 | 304 | 400 | 401 | 403 | 404 | 'customCode' | Expression<number>;
      /** Code
       * @displayOptions.show { responseCode: ["customCode"] }
       * @default 200
       */
      customCode?: number | Expression<number>;
    };
  };
    /** Set a custom content-type to return if another one as the "application/json" should be returned
     * @displayOptions.show { /responseData: ["firstEntryJson"], /responseMode: ["lastNode"] }
     */
    responseContentType?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
    /** Custom response data to send
     * @displayOptions.show { /responseMode: ["onReceived"] }
     * @displayOptions.hide { noResponseBody: [true] }
     */
    responseData?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
    /** Add headers to the webhook response
     * @default {}
     */
    responseHeaders?: {
        /** Entries
     */
    entries?: Array<{
      /** Name of the header
       */
      name?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
      /** Value of the header
       */
      value?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
    }>;
  };
  };
}

export type WebhookV21Output = {
  headers?: Record<string, unknown>;
  params?: Record<string, unknown>;
  query?: Record<string, unknown>;
  body?: Record<string, unknown>;
  webhookUrl?: string;
  executionMode?: string;
};

export interface WebhookV21Credentials {
  httpBasicAuth: CredentialReference;
  httpHeaderAuth: CredentialReference;
  jwtAuth: CredentialReference;
}

interface WebhookV21NodeBase {
  type: 'n8n-nodes-base.webhook';
  version: 2.1;
  credentials?: WebhookV21Credentials;
  isTrigger: true;
}

export type WebhookV21ParamsNode = WebhookV21NodeBase & {
  config: NodeConfig<WebhookV21Params>;
  output?: Items<WebhookV21Output>;
};

export type WebhookV21Node = WebhookV21ParamsNode;
```

---

## n8n-nodes-base.respondToWebhook (v15)

```typescript
/**
 * Respond to Webhook Node - Version 1.5
 * Returns data for Webhook
 */


export interface RespondToWebhookV15Params {
/**
 * Whether to provide an additional output branch with the response sent to the webhook
 * @default false
 */
    enableResponseOutput?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
/**
 * The data that should be returned
 * @default firstIncomingItem
 */
    respondWith?: 'allIncomingItems' | 'binary' | 'firstIncomingItem' | 'json' | 'jwt' | 'noData' | 'redirect' | 'text';
/**
 * The URL to redirect to
 * @displayOptions.show { respondWith: ["redirect"] }
 */
    redirectURL?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
/**
 * The HTTP response JSON data
 * @displayOptions.show { respondWith: ["json", "text"] }
 */
    responseBody?: IDataObject | string | Expression<string>;
/**
 * The payload to include in the JWT token
 * @displayOptions.show { respondWith: ["jwt"] }
 */
    payload?: IDataObject | string | Expression<string>;
/**
 * Response Data Source
 * @displayOptions.show { respondWith: ["binary"] }
 * @default automatically
 */
    responseDataSource?: 'automatically' | 'set' | Expression<string>;
/**
 * The name of the node input field with the binary data
 * @displayOptions.show { respondWith: ["binary"], responseDataSource: ["set"] }
 * @default data
 */
    inputFieldName?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
  options?: {
    /** The HTTP response code to return. Defaults to 200.
     * @default 200
     */
    responseCode?: number | Expression<number>;
    /** Add headers to the webhook response
     * @default {}
     */
    responseHeaders?: {
        /** Entries
     */
    entries?: Array<{
      /** Name of the header
       */
      name?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
      /** Value of the header
       */
      value?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
    }>;
  };
    /** The name of the response field to put all items in
     * @displayOptions.show { /respondWith: ["allIncomingItems", "firstIncomingItem"] }
     */
    responseKey?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
    /** Whether to enable streaming to the response
     * @displayOptions.show { /respondWith: ["allIncomingItems", "firstIncomingItem", "text", "json", "jwt"] }
     * @default true
     */
    enableStreaming?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
  };
}

export interface RespondToWebhookV15Credentials {
  jwtAuth: CredentialReference;
}

interface RespondToWebhookV15NodeBase {
  type: 'n8n-nodes-base.respondToWebhook';
  version: 1.5;
  credentials?: RespondToWebhookV15Credentials;
}

export type RespondToWebhookV15ParamsNode = RespondToWebhookV15NodeBase & {
  config: NodeConfig<RespondToWebhookV15Params>;
};

export type RespondToWebhookV15Node = RespondToWebhookV15ParamsNode;
```

## n8n-nodes-base.dataTable (v11)

```typescript
/**
 * Data table Node - Version 1.1
 * Discriminator: resource=row, operation=get
 */


/** Get row(s) */
export type DataTableV11RowGetParams = {
  resource: 'row';
  operation: 'get';
/**
 * Data table
 * @builderHint Default to mode: 'list' which is easier for users to set up
 * @default {"mode":"list","value":""}
 */
    dataTableId?: { __rl: true; mode: 'list' | 'name' | 'id'; value: string; cachedResultName?: string };
/**
 * Must Match
 * @default anyCondition
 */
    matchType?: 'anyCondition' | 'allConditions' | Expression<string>;
/**
 * Filter to decide which rows get
 * @default {}
 */
    filters?: {
        /** Conditions
     */
    conditions?: Array<{
      /** Choose from the list, or specify using an &lt;a href="https://docs.n8n.io/code/expressions/"&gt;expression&lt;/a&gt;
       * @default id
       */
      keyName?: string | Expression<string>;
      /** Condition
       * @default eq
       */
      condition?: string | Expression<string>;
      /** Value
       * @displayOptions.hide { condition: ["isEmpty", "isNotEmpty", "isTrue", "isFalse"] }
       */
      keyValue?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
    }>;
  };
/**
 * Whether to return all results or only up to a given limit
 * @default false
 */
    returnAll?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
/**
 * Max number of results to return
 * @displayOptions.show { returnAll: [false] }
 * @default 50
 */
    limit?: number | Expression<number>;
/**
 * Whether to sort the results by a column
 * @default false
 */
    orderBy?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
/**
 * Choose from the list, or specify using an &lt;a href="https://docs.n8n.io/code/expressions/"&gt;expression&lt;/a&gt;
 * @displayOptions.show { orderBy: [true] }
 * @default createdAt
 */
    orderByColumn?: string | Expression<string>;
/**
 * Sort direction for the column
 * @displayOptions.show { orderBy: [true] }
 * @default DESC
 */
    orderByDirection?: 'ASC' | 'DESC' | Expression<string>;
};

export type DataTableV11RowGetOutput = {
  createdAt?: string;
  id?: number;
  updatedAt?: string;
};

export type DataTableV11RowGetNode = {
  type: 'n8n-nodes-base.dataTable';
  version: 1.1;
  config: NodeConfig<DataTableV11RowGetParams>;
  output?: Items<DataTableV11RowGetOutput>;
};
```

---

## n8n-nodes-base.dataTable (v11)

```typescript
/**
 * Data table Node - Version 1.1
 * Discriminator: resource=row, operation=insert
 */


/** Insert a new row */
export type DataTableV11RowInsertParams = {
  resource: 'row';
  operation: 'insert';
/**
 * Data table
 * @builderHint Default to mode: 'list' which is easier for users to set up
 * @default {"mode":"list","value":""}
 */
    dataTableId?: { __rl: true; mode: 'list' | 'name' | 'id'; value: string; cachedResultName?: string };
/**
 * Columns
 * @default {"mappingMode":"defineBelow","value":null}
 */
    columns?: string;
/**
 * Options
 * @default {}
 */
    options?: {
    /** Whether to improve bulk insert performance 5x by not returning inserted data
     * @default false
     */
    optimizeBulk?: boolean;
  };
};

export type DataTableV11RowInsertOutput = {
  createdAt?: string;
  id?: number;
  updatedAt?: string;
};

export type DataTableV11RowInsertNode = {
  type: 'n8n-nodes-base.dataTable';
  version: 1.1;
  config: NodeConfig<DataTableV11RowInsertParams>;
  output?: Items<DataTableV11RowInsertOutput>;
};
```

## n8n-nodes-base.html (v12)

```typescript
/**
 * HTML Node - Version 1.2
 * Work with HTML
 */


export interface HtmlV12Params {
  operation?: 'generateHtmlTemplate' | 'extractHtmlContent' | 'convertToHtmlTable';
/**
 * HTML template to render
 * @builderHint Use expressions to generate loops, reference data, etc. Does not support handlebars.
 * @displayOptions.show { operation: ["generateHtmlTemplate"] }
 */
    html?: string;
/**
 * If HTML should be read from binary or JSON data
 * @displayOptions.show { operation: ["extractHtmlContent"] }
 * @default json
 */
    sourceData?: 'binary' | 'json' | Expression<string>;
/**
 * Input Binary Field
 * @hint The name of the input binary field containing the file to be extracted
 * @displayOptions.show { operation: ["extractHtmlContent"], sourceData: ["binary", "json"] }
 * @default data
 */
    dataPropertyName?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
/**
 * Extraction Values
 * @displayOptions.show { operation: ["extractHtmlContent"] }
 * @default {"values":[{"key":"","cssSelector":"","returnValue":"text","returnArray":false}]}
 */
    extractionValues?: {
        /** Values
     */
    values?: Array<{
      /** The key under which the extracted value should be saved
       */
      key?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
      /** The CSS selector to use
       */
      cssSelector?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
      /** What kind of data should be returned
       * @default text
       */
      returnValue?: 'attribute' | 'html' | 'text' | 'value' | Expression<string>;
      /** The name of the attribute to return the value off
       * @displayOptions.show { returnValue: ["attribute"] }
       */
      attribute?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
      /** Comma-separated list of selectors to skip in the text extraction
       * @displayOptions.show { returnValue: ["text"] }
       */
      skipSelectors?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
      /** Whether to return the values as an array so if multiple ones get found they also get returned separately. If not set all will be returned as a single string.
       * @default false
       */
      returnArray?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
    }>;
  };
/**
 * Options
 * @displayOptions.show { operation: ["extractHtmlContent", "convertToHtmlTable"] }
 * @default {}
 */
    options?: {
    /** Whether to remove automatically all spaces and newlines from the beginning and end of the values
     * @default true
     */
    trimValues?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
    /** Whether to remove leading and trailing whitespaces, line breaks (newlines) and condense multiple consecutive whitespaces into a single space
     * @default true
     */
    cleanUpText?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
    /** Whether to capitalize the headers
     * @default false
     */
    capitalize?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
    /** Whether to use custom styling
     * @default false
     */
    customStyling?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
    /** Caption to add to the table
     */
    caption?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
    /** Attributes to attach to the table
     */
    tableAttributes?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
    /** Attributes to attach to the table header
     */
    headerAttributes?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
    /** Attributes to attach to the table row
     */
    rowAttributes?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
    /** Attributes to attach to the table cell
     */
    cellAttributes?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
  };
}

interface HtmlV12NodeBase {
  type: 'n8n-nodes-base.html';
  version: 1.2;
}

export type HtmlV12ParamsNode = HtmlV12NodeBase & {
  config: NodeConfig<HtmlV12Params>;
};

export type HtmlV12Node = HtmlV12ParamsNode;
```

---

## n8n-nodes-base.if (v23)

```typescript
/**
 * If Node - Version 2.3
 * Route items to different branches (true/false)
 */


// Helper types for special n8n fields
type FilterValue = { conditions: Array<{ leftValue: unknown; operator: { type: string; operation: string }; rightValue: unknown }> };

export interface IfV23Params {
  conditions?: FilterValue;
/**
 * If the type of an expression doesn't match the type of the comparison, n8n will try to cast the expression to the required type. E.g. for booleans &lt;code&gt;"false"&lt;/code&gt; or &lt;code&gt;0&lt;/code&gt; will be cast to &lt;code&gt;false&lt;/code&gt;
 * @default false
 */
    looseTypeValidation?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
  options?: {
    /** Whether to ignore letter case when evaluating conditions
     * @default true
     */
    ignoreCase?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
    /** If the type of an expression doesn't match the type of the comparison, n8n will try to cast the expression to the required type. E.g. for booleans &lt;code&gt;"false"&lt;/code&gt; or &lt;code&gt;0&lt;/code&gt; will be cast to &lt;code&gt;false&lt;/code&gt;
     * @default true
     */
    looseTypeValidation?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
  };
}

interface IfV23NodeBase {
  type: 'n8n-nodes-base.if';
  version: 2.3;
}

export type IfV23ParamsNode = IfV23NodeBase & {
  config: NodeConfig<IfV23Params>;
};

export type IfV23Node = IfV23ParamsNode;
```

---

## n8n-nodes-base.set (v34)

```typescript
/**
 * Edit Fields (Set) Node - Version 3.4
 * Discriminator: mode=manual
 */


// Helper types for special n8n fields
/**
 * Assignment type determines how the value is interpreted.
 * - string: Direct string value or expression evaluating to string
 * - number: Direct number value or expression evaluating to number
 * - boolean: Direct boolean value or expression evaluating to boolean
 * - array: Expression that evaluates to an array, e.g. ={{ [1, 2, 3] }} or ={{ $json.items }}
 * - object: Expression that evaluates to a plain object (not an array — use the array type for arrays), e.g. ={{ { key: 'value' } }} or ={{ $json.data }}
 * - binary: Property name of binary data in the input item, or expression to access binary data from previous nodes, e.g. ={{ $('Node').item.binary.data }}
 */
type AssignmentType = 'string' | 'number' | 'boolean' | 'array' | 'object' | 'binary';
type AssignmentCollectionValue = { assignments: Array<{ id: string; name: string; value: unknown; type: AssignmentType }> };

/** Edit item fields one by one */
export type SetV34ManualParams = {
  mode: 'manual';
/**
 * Whether this item should be duplicated a set number of times
 * @default false
 */
    duplicateItem?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
/**
 * How many times the item should be duplicated, mainly used for testing and debugging
 * @displayOptions.show { duplicateItem: [true] }
 * @default 0
 */
    duplicateCount?: number | Expression<number>;
/**
 * Fields to Set
 * @default {}
 */
    assignments?: AssignmentCollectionValue;
/**
 * Whether to pass to the output all the input fields (along with the fields set in 'Fields to Set')
 * @default false
 */
    includeOtherFields?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
/**
 * How to select the fields you want to include in your output items
 * @displayOptions.hide { /includeOtherFields: [false] }
 * @default all
 */
    include?: 'all' | 'selected' | 'except' | Expression<string>;
/**
 * Comma-separated list of the field names you want to include in the output. You can drag the selected fields from the input panel.
 * @displayOptions.show { include: ["selected"], /includeOtherFields: [true] }
 */
    includeFields?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
/**
 * Comma-separated list of the field names you want to exclude from the output. You can drag the selected fields from the input panel.
 * @displayOptions.show { include: ["except"], /includeOtherFields: [true] }
 */
    excludeFields?: string | Expression<string> | PlaceholderValue;
  options?: {
    /** Whether binary data should be included if present in the input item
     * @default true
     */
    includeBinary?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
    /** Whether binary data should be stripped from the input item. Only applies when "Include Other Input Fields" is enabled.
     * @displayOptions.show { /includeOtherFields: [true] }
     * @default true
     */
    stripBinary?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
    /** Whether to ignore field type errors and apply a less strict type conversion
     * @default false
     */
    ignoreConversionErrors?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
    /** By default, dot-notation is used in property names. This means that "a.b" will set the property "b" underneath "a" so { "a": { "b": value} }. If that is not intended this can be deactivated, it will then set { "a.b": value } instead.
     * @default true
     */
    dotNotation?: boolean | Expression<boolean>;
  };
};

export type SetV34ManualNode = {
  type: 'n8n-nodes-base.set';
  version: 3.4;
  config: NodeConfig<SetV34ManualParams>;
};
```

        '<head>\n' +
        '  <title>Contacts</title>\n' +
        '  <style>\n' +
        '    body { font-family: Arial, sans-serif; margin: 40px; }\n' +
        '    h1 { color: #333; }\n' +
        '    table { border-collapse: collapse; width: 100%; margin-top: 20px; }\n' +
        '    th, td { border: 1px solid #ddd; padding: 12px; text-align: left; }\n' +
        '    th { background-color: #4CAF50; color: white; }\n' +
        '    tr:nth-child(even) { background-color: #f2f2f2; }\n' +
        '    .actions { margin-top: 20px; }\n' +
        '    a { color: #4CAF50; text-decoration: none; }\n' +
        '  </style>\n' +
        '</head>\n' +
        '<body>\n' +
        '  <h1>Contacts</h1>\n' +
        '  <div class="actions">\n' +
        '    <a href="/webhook/contacts/new">Add New Contact</a>\n' +
        '  </div>\n' +
        '  <table>\n' +
        '    <thead>\n' +

        '        <th>Company</th>\n' +
        '      </tr>\n' +

        '        <tr>\n' +

        '          <td>${item.json.email}</td>\n' +
        '          <td>${item.json.phone}</td>\n' +

      "parameters": {
        "operation": "generateHtmlTemplate",
        "html": "=<!DOCTYPE html>\n<html>\n<head>\n  <title>Contacts</title>\n  <style>\n    body { font-family: Arial, sans-serif; margin: 40px; }\n    h1 { color: #333; }\n    table { border-collapse: collapse; width: 100%; margin-top: 20px; }\n    th, td { border: 1px solid #ddd; padding: 12px; text-align: left; }\n    th { background-color: #4CAF50; color: white; }\n    tr:nth-child(even) { background-color: #f2f2f2; }\n    .actions { margin-top: 20px; }\n    a { color: #4CAF50; text-decoration: none; }\n  </style>\n</head>\n<body>\n  <h1>Contacts</h1>\n  <div class=\"actions\">\n    <a href=\"/webhook/contacts/new\">Add New Contact</a>\n  </div>\n  <table>\n    <thead>\n      <tr>\n        <th>Name</th>\n        <th>Email</th>\n        <th>Phone</th>\n        <th>Company</th>\n      </tr>\n    </thead>\n    <tbody>\n      {{ $(\"Fetch All Contacts\").all().map(item => `\n        <tr>\n          <td>${item.json.name}</td>\n          <td>${item.json.email}</td>\n          <td>${item.json.phone}</td>\n          <td>${item.json.company}</td>\n        </tr>\n      `).join(\"\") }}\n    </tbody>\n  </table>\n</body>\n</html>"
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "a5968283-b0f2-46f6-83de-6966a862fb80",
      "name": "Respond Contacts List",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.respondToWebhook",
      "typeVersion": 1.5,
      "position": [
        1140,
        300
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "respondWith": "text",
        "responseBody": "={{ $json.html }}",
        "options": {
          "responseHeaders": {
            "entries": [
              {
                "name": "Content-Type",
                "value": "text/html"
              }
            ]
          }
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "941d90c8-cea2-41f2-8b50-75471520a669",
      "name": "GET /contacts/new",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.webhook",
      "typeVersion": 2.1,
      "position": [
        240,
        600
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "httpMethod": "GET",

      }

    {
      "id": "74ac07a4-35f6-4fb2-ad82-e3b07b15b298",
      "name": "Generate New Contact Form",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.html",
      "typeVersion": 1.2,
      "position": [
        540,
        600
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "operation": "generateHtmlTemplate",
        "html": "=<!DOCTYPE html>\n<html>\n<head>\n  <title>New Contact</title>\n  <style>\n    body { font-family: Arial, sans-serif; margin: 40px; }\n    h1 { color: #333; }\n    form { max-width: 500px; }\n    label { display: block; margin-top: 15px; font-weight: bold; }\n    input { width: 100%; padding: 8px; margin-top: 5px; border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 4px; }\n    button { margin-top: 20px; padding: 10px 20px; background-color: #4CAF50; color: white; border: none; border-radius: 4px; cursor: pointer; }\n    button:hover { background-color: #45a049; }\n    .back { margin-top: 20px; }\n    a { color: #4CAF50; text-decoration: none; }\n  </style>\n</head>\n<body>\n  <h1>Add New Contact</h1>\n  <form action=\"/webhook/contacts\" method=\"POST\">\n    <label for=\"name\">Name:</label>\n    <input type=\"text\" id=\"name\" name=\"name\" required>\n    \n    <label for=\"email\">Email:</label>\n    <input type=\"email\" id=\"email\" name=\"email\" required>\n    \n    <label for=\"phone\">Phone:</label>\n    <input type=\"tel\" id=\"phone\" name=\"phone\" required>\n    \n    <label for=\"company\">Company:</label>\n    <input type=\"text\" id=\"company\" name=\"company\" required>\n    \n    <button type=\"submit\">Add Contact</button>\n  </form>\n  <div class=\"back\">\n    <a href=\"/webhook/contacts\">Back to Contacts</a>\n  </div>\n</body>\n</html>"
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "efbc7cdc-2201-4a33-9ae4-864209c4f0fe",
      "name": "Respond New Contact Form",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.respondToWebhook",
      "typeVersion": 1.5,
      "position": [
        840,
        600
      ],
      "parameters": {

          "responseHeaders": {

              }
            ]
          }

            },

      }

    {
      "id": "d7993f2f-70b9-4513-ba63-8613ec9a6d94",
      "name": "Generate Success HTML",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.html",
      "typeVersion": 1.2,
      "position": [
        1140,
        800
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "operation": "generateHtmlTemplate",
        "html": "=<!DOCTYPE html>\n<html>\n<head>\n  <title>Contact Added</title>\n  <style>\n    body { font-family: Arial, sans-serif; margin: 40px; }\n    .success { color: #4CAF50; }\n    .info { background-color: #f0f0f0; padding: 15px; border-radius: 4px; margin-top: 20px; }\n    a { color: #4CAF50; text-decoration: none; }\n  </style>\n</head>\n<body>\n  <h1 class=\"success\">Contact Added Successfully!</h1>\n  <div class=\"info\">\n    <p><strong>Name:</strong> {{ $json.name }}</p>\n    <p><strong>Email:</strong> {{ $json.email }}</p>\n    <p><strong>Phone:</strong> {{ $json.phone }}</p>\n    <p><strong>Company:</strong> {{ $json.company }}</p>\n  </div>\n  <p><a href=\"/webhook/contacts\">View All Contacts</a></p>\n</body>\n</html>"
      }
    },

COMPLEX Create a workflow that receives an HTTP POST at /onboarding with new employee da... webhook parallel wait PASS 9/9 (100%) 5 get_suggested_nodes search_nodes get_node_types str_replace_based_edit_tool batch_str_replace 46.5s 25 3,772 280

Prompt

Create a workflow that receives an HTTP POST at /onboarding with new employee data. First, create accounts in parallel: email account, Slack workspace, and project management tool. After all three accounts are created, send a welcome email with all the account details. Then set a 7-day timer wait. After the wait, send a follow-up survey to check how onboarding went.

Workflow Preview

Checklist (9/9)

  • ✓ [structure] Has HTTP POST trigger at /onboarding
    The webhook node has httpMethod 'POST' and path 'onboarding', satisfying the HTTP POST trigger at /onboarding requirement.
  • ✓ [structure] Creates email account, Slack workspace account, and project management tool account in parallel (3 concurrent branches)
    The 'Receive Employee Data' node connects to all three account creation nodes (Create Email Account, Create Slack Account, Create Project Management Account) in a single connection array, meaning they are triggered in parallel as 3 concurrent branches.
  • ✓ [structure] All 3 parallel account creation branches must complete before proceeding (join/merge step)
    The 'Combine Account Details' merge node receives inputs from all three account creation branches (index 0, 1, and 2), acting as a join/merge step that requires all three to complete before proceeding.
  • ✓ [structure] Sends a welcome email after all accounts are created
    The 'Send Welcome Email' Gmail node is connected after 'Combine Account Details' and sends an email to the employee.
  • ✓ [structure] Has a 7-day timer wait after the welcome email
    The 'Wait 7 Days' node uses resume 'timeInterval' with amount 7 and unit 'days', implementing a 7-day timer wait after the welcome email.
  • ✓ [structure] Sends a follow-up survey after the 7-day wait
    The 'Send Follow-up Survey' Gmail node is connected after 'Wait 7 Days' and sends a follow-up survey email.
  • ✓ [data] Receives new employee data from the POST request body
    The webhook node receives POST data and subsequent nodes reference $json.body.email, $json.body.firstName, $json.body.lastName, and $json.body.department from the request body.
  • ✓ [data] Welcome email includes details for all three created accounts (email, Slack, and project management tool)
    The welcome email message template includes references to email account details ($json.email), Slack account details ($json.userId), and project management account details ($json.accountId), covering all three created accounts.
  • ✓ [data] Follow-up survey is targeted to the onboarded employee to check how onboarding went
    The follow-up survey email is sent to the employee's email address (from the original POST body) with subject 'How is your onboarding going?' and message content specifically asking about their onboarding experience, targeting the onboarded employee.

Generated Code (280 lines)

{
  "id": "employee-onboarding",
  "name": "Employee Onboarding Workflow",
  "nodes": [
    {
      "id": "b8f5c6d8-6c63-44db-b552-830091a49861",
      "name": "Receive Employee Data",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.webhook",
      "typeVersion": 2.1,
      "position": [
        240,
        400
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "httpMethod": "POST",
        "path": "onboarding",
        "responseMode": "onReceived"
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "43c164a1-d0dc-4872-b57c-05a26c181db4",
      "name": "Create Email Account",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest",
      "typeVersion": 4.4,
      "position": [
        540,
        200
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "method": "POST",
        "url": "<__PLACEHOLDER_VALUE__Email provisioning API URL__>",
        "sendBody": true,
        "contentType": "json",
        "specifyBody": "json",
        "jsonBody": "={{ { \"email\": $json.body.email, \"firstName\": $json.body.firstName, \"lastName\": $json.body.lastName } }}"
      },
      "executeOnce": true
    },
    {
      "id": "7a36e5a1-62f8-4603-9186-45c17e4964c3",
      "name": "Combine Account Details",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.merge",
      "typeVersion": 3.2,
      "position": [
        840,
        400
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "mode": "combine",
        "combineBy": "combineByPosition"
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "59ca3bca-d902-420c-a133-1d86b349b98c",
      "name": "Create Slack Account",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest",
      "typeVersion": 4.4,
      "position": [
        540,
        400
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "method": "POST",
        "url": "<__PLACEHOLDER_VALUE__Slack user provisioning API URL__>",
        "sendBody": true,
        "contentType": "json",
        "specifyBody": "json",
        "jsonBody": "={{ { \"email\": $json.body.email, \"real_name\": $json.body.firstName + \" \" + $json.body.lastName } }}"
      },
      "executeOnce": true
    },
    {
      "id": "d37fe3ac-63a1-49ab-9c96-2383dff309a6",
      "name": "Create Project Management Account",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest",
      "typeVersion": 4.4,
      "position": [
        540,
        600
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "method": "POST",
        "url": "<__PLACEHOLDER_VALUE__Project management tool API URL__>",
        "sendBody": true,
        "contentType": "json",
        "specifyBody": "json",
        "jsonBody": "={{ { \"email\": $json.body.email, \"name\": $json.body.firstName + \" \" + $json.body.lastName, \"department\": $json.body.department } }}"
      },
      "executeOnce": true
    },
    {
      "id": "f61949b5-957b-4f42-8ac0-0321dc3f13e6",
      "name": "Send Welcome Email",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.gmail",
      "typeVersion": 2.2,
      "position": [
        1140,
        400
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "resource": "message",
        "operation": "send",
        "sendTo": "={{ $(\"Receive Employee Data\").item.json.body.email }}",
        "subject": "Welcome to the Team!",
        "emailType": "html",
        "message": "=Hi {{ $(\"Receive Employee Data\").item.json.body.firstName }},\n\nWelcome to the team! Your accounts have been created:\n\n- Email: {{ $json.email }}\n- Slack User ID: {{ $json.userId }}\n- Project Management Account: {{ $json.accountId }}\n\nBest regards,\nHR Team"
      },
      "credentials": {}
    },
    {
      "id": "cbfe4834-f6bd-4333-8269-a3b0d362093c",
      "name": "Wait 7 Days",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.wait",
      "typeVersion": 1.1,
      "position": [
        1440,
        400
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "resume": "timeInterval",
        "amount": 7,
        "unit": "days"
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "eba38c8f-7ca3-42be-be23-9feba67d2789",
      "name": "Send Follow-up Survey",
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.gmail",
      "typeVersion": 2.2,
      "position": [
        1740,
        400
      ],
      "parameters": {
        "resource": "message",
        "operation": "send",
        "sendTo": "={{ $(\"Receive Employee Data\").item.json.body.email }}",
        "subject": "How is your onboarding going?",
        "emailType": "html",
        "message": "=Hi {{ $(\"Receive Employee Data\").item.json.body.firstName }},\n\nIt has been a week since you joined! We would love to hear about your onboarding experience.\n\nPlease take a moment to complete this brief survey: [Survey Link]\n\nThank you,\nHR Team"
      },
      "credentials": {}
    }
  ],
  "connections": {
    "Receive Employee Data": {
      "main": [
        [
          {
            "node": "Create Email Account",
            "type": "main",
            "index": 0
          },
          {
            "node": "Create Slack Account",
            "type": "main",
            "index": 0
          },
          {
            "node": "Create Project Management Account",
            "type": "main",
            "index": 0
          }
        ]
      ]
    },
    "Create Email Account": {
      "main": [
        [
          {
            "node": "Combine Account Details",
            "type": "main",
            "index": 0
          }
        ]
      ]
    },
    "Combine Account Details": {
      "main": [
        [
          {
            "node": "Send Welcome Email",
            "type": "main",
            "index": 0
          }
        ]
      ]
    },
    "Create Slack Account": {
      "main": [
        [
          {
            "node": "Combine Account Details",
            "type": "main",
            "index": 1
          }
        ]
      ]
    },
    "Create Project Management Account": {
      "main": [
        [
          {
            "node": "Combine Account Details",
            "type": "main",
            "index": 2
          }
        ]
      ]
    },
    "Send Welcome Email": {
      "main": [
        [
          {
            "node": "Wait 7 Days",
            "type": "main",
            "index": 0
          }
        ]
      ]
    },
    "Wait 7 Days": {
      "main": [
        [
          {
            "node": "Send Follow-up Survey",
            "type": "main",
            "index": 0
          }
        ]
      ]
    }
  },
  "settings": {},
  "pinData": {
    "Receive Employee Data": [
      {
        "body": {
          "firstName": "John",
          "lastName": "Doe",
          "email": "john.doe@company.com",
          "department": "Engineering"
        }

    ],

#1 - · 3 in · 152 out · 0 thinking · 0 tool calls

get_suggested_nodes OK
Input
{
  "categories": [
    "notification",
    "scheduling"
  ]
}
Output
## notification
patternHint: Trigger → Condition → Send (Email/Slack/SMS)

Suggested nodes:
- n8n-nodes-base.webhook
  displayName: Webhook
  description: Starts the workflow when a webhook is called
  note: Event-based notifications from external systems
- n8n-nodes-base.scheduleTrigger
  displayName: Schedule Trigger
  description: Triggers the workflow on a given schedule
  note: Periodic monitoring and batch notifications
- n8n-nodes-base.gmail
  displayName: Gmail
  description: Consume the Gmail API
  note: Default to this because it's easy for users to setup
- n8n-nodes-base.slack
  displayName: Slack
  description: Consume Slack API
- n8n-nodes-base.telegram
  displayName: Telegram
  description: Sends data to Telegram
- n8n-nodes-base.twilio
  displayName: Twilio
  description: Send SMS and WhatsApp messages or make phone calls
- n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest
  displayName: HTTP Request
  description: Makes an HTTP request and returns the response data
  note: For services without dedicated nodes (Teams, Discord)
- n8n-nodes-base.if
  displayName: If
  description: Route items to different branches (true/false)
  note: Check alert conditions before sending
- n8n-nodes-base.switch
  displayName: Switch
  description: Route items depending on defined expression or rules
  note: If routing by severity/type is needed, use Switch to direct to different channels

## scheduling
patternHint: Schedule Trigger → Fetch → Process → Act

Suggested nodes:
- n8n-nodes-base.scheduleTrigger
  displayName: Schedule Trigger
  description: Triggers the workflow on a given schedule
- n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest
  displayName: HTTP Request
  description: Makes an HTTP request and returns the response data
- n8n-nodes-base.set
  displayName: Edit Fields (Set)
  description: Modify, add, or remove item fields
- n8n-nodes-base.wait
  displayName: Wait
  description: Wait before continue with execution
  note: Respect rate limits between API calls
search_nodes OK
Input
{
  "queries": [
    "webhook",
    "gmail",
    "slack",
    "email",
    "wait",
    "merge",
    "http request",
    "set"
  ]
}

  Discriminators: none (use node directly without resource/operation/mode)

  Version: 1

    resource:
      - list:
          operations:
      - task:
          operations:

## "slack"
Found 5 nodes:

- n8n-nodes-base.slack
  Display Name: Slack
  Version: 2.4
  Description: Consume Slack API
  Discriminators:
    resource:
      - channel:
          operations:
            - archive
              Archives a conversation
            - close
              Closes a direct message or multi-person direct message
            - create
              Initiates a public or private channel-based conversation
            - get
              Get information about a channel
            - getAll
              Get many channels in a Slack team
            - history
              Get a conversation's history of messages and events
            - invite
              Invite a user to a channel
            - join
              Joins an existing conversation
            - kick
              Removes a user from a channel
            - leave
              Leaves a conversation
            - member
              List members of a conversation
            - open
              Opens or resumes a direct message or multi-person direct message
            - rename
              Renames a conversation
            - replies
              Get a thread of messages posted to a channel
            - setPurpose
              Sets the purpose for a conversation
            - setTopic
              Sets the topic for a conversation
            - unarchive
              Unarchives a conversation
      - file:
          operations:
            - get
            - getAll
              Get & filters team files
            - upload
              Create or upload an existing file
      - message:
          operations:
            - delete
            - getPermalink
            - search
            - post
            - sendAndWait
            - update
      - reaction:
          operations:
            - add
              Adds a reaction to a message
            - get
              Get the reactions of a message
            - remove
              Remove a reaction of a message
      - star:
          operations:
            - add
              Add a star to an item
            - delete
              Delete a star from an item
            - getAll
              Get many stars of autenticated user
      - user:
          operations:
            - info
              Get information about a user
            - getAll
              Get a list of many users
            - getProfile
              Get a user's profile
            - getPresence
              Get online status of a user
            - updateProfile
              Update a user's profile
      - userGroup:
          operations:
            - updateUsers
            - create
            - disable
            - enable
            - getAll
            - getUsers
            - update

  Use get_node_types with discriminators:

## "merge"
Found 5 nodes:

- n8n-nodes-base.merge
  Display Name: Merge
  Version: 3.2
  Description: Merges data of multiple streams once data from both is available
  Discriminators:
    mode:
      - append: "Append"
        Output items of each input, one after the other
        @builderHint Append items from multiple branches into a single list sequentially. Waits for all running branches. Supports any number of inputs. @example input1: [{ x }] [{ y }] input2: [{ z }]. Output: [{ x }, { y }, { z }]. Next node will execute 3 times with each item. Set executeOnce on next node to execute once.
      - combine: "Combine"
        Merge matching items together
        @builderHint Combines items from 2 branches. Waits for both to have input data. @example **combine by position** input1: [{ x }, { y }] input2: [{ z }] output: [{ x, y }, { x: undefined, y: undefined, z }] @example **combine by key** input1: [{ id: 1, x }, { id: 2, y }] input2: [{ id: 1, z }] output: [{ id: 1, x, z }, { id: 2, y }]
      - combineBySql: "SQL Query"
        Write a query to do the merge
        @builderHint Need to combine more than 2 branches? Use SQL Query for advanced operations. Waits for all inputs. @example Results depend on query - can filter, join, aggregate
      - chooseBranch: "Choose Branch"
        Output data from a specific branch, without modifying it
        @builderHint Do you need to select data from only ONE specific input and discard the others? Use Choose Branch after conditional nodes to pick which path to continue. Waits for all inputs. @example 3 items from Input A + 2 items from Input B, choose Input A → 3 items

  Use get_node_types with discriminators:
    get_node_types({ nodeIds: [{ nodeId: "n8n-nodes-base.merge", mode: "append" }] })

- n8n-nodes-base.mailerLiteTrigger [TRIGGER]
  Display Name: MailerLite Trigger
  Version: 2
  Description: Starts the workflow when MailerLite events occur
  Discriminators: none (use node directly without resource/operation/mode)

- n8n-nodes-base.emeliaTrigger [TRIGGER]

  Description: Handle Emelia campaign activity events via webhooks
  Discriminators: none (use node directly without resource/operation/mode)

- n8n-nodes-base.customerIoTrigger [TRIGGER]
  Display Name: Customer.io Trigger
  Version: 1
  Description: Starts the workflow on a Customer.io update (Beta)
  Discriminators: none (use node directly without resource/operation/mode)

## "http request"
Found 2 nodes:

- n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest
  Display Name: HTTP Request
  Version: 4.4
  Description: Makes an HTTP request and returns the response data
  @builderHint Prefer dedicated integration nodes over HTTP Request — n8n has 400+ dedicated nodes (e.g. Gmail, Slack, Google Sheets, Notion, OpenAI, HubSpot, Jira, etc.) with built-in authentication, pre-configured parameters, better error handling, and easier maintenance. Only use HTTP Request when no dedicated node exists for the service, the user explicitly requests it, accessing a custom/internal API, or the dedicated node does not support the specific operation needed.
  Discriminators: none (use node directly without resource/operation/mode)

- n8n-nodes-base.httpRequestTool
  Display Name: HTTP Request Tool
  Version: 4.4
  Description: Makes an HTTP request and returns the response data
  @builderHint Prefer dedicated integration nodes over HTTP Request — n8n has 400+ dedicated nodes (e.g. Gmail, Slack, Google Sheets, Notion, OpenAI, HubSpot, Jira, etc.) with built-in authentication, pre-configured parameters, better error handling, and easier maintenance. Only use HTTP Request when no dedicated node exists for the service, the user explicitly requests it, accessing a custom/internal API, or the dedicated node does not support the specific operation needed.
  Discriminators: none (use node directly without resource/operation/mode)

## "set"
Found 5 nodes:

- n8n-nodes-base.set
  Display Name: Edit Fields (Set)
  Version: 3.4
  Description: Modify, add, or remove item fields
  Discriminators:
    mode:
      - manual: "Manual Mapping"
        Edit item fields one by one
      - raw: "JSON"
        Customize item output with JSON

  Use get_node_types with discriminators:
    get_node_types({ nodeIds: [{ nodeId: "n8n-nodes-base.set", mode: "manual" }] })

- n8n-nodes-base.seaTable
  Display Name: SeaTable
  Version: 2
  Description: Consume the SeaTable API
  Discriminators:
    resource:
      - row:
          operations:
            - create
              Create a new row
            - remove
              Delete a row
            - get
              Get the content of a row
            - list
              Get many rows from a table or a table view
            - lock
              Lock a row to prevent further changes
            - search
              Search one or multiple rows
            - unlock
              Remove the lock from a row
            - update
              Update the content of a row
      - base:
          operations:
            - snapshot
              Create a snapshot of the base
            - metadata
              Get the complete metadata of the base
            - collaborator
              Get the username from the email or name of a collaborator
      - link:
          operations:
            - add
              Create a link between two rows in a link column
            - list
              List all links of a specific row
            - remove
              Remove a link between two rows from a link column
      - asset:
          operations:
            - getPublicURL
              Get the public URL from asset path
            - upload
              Add a file/image to an existing row

  Use get_node_types with discriminators:
    get_node_types({ nodeIds: [{ nodeId: "n8n-nodes-base.seaTable", resource: "row", operation: "create" }] })

- n8n-nodes-base.sendyTool
  Display Name: Sendy Tool
  Version: 1
  Description: Consume Sendy API
  Discriminators:
    resource:
      - campaign:
          operations:
            - create
              Create a campaign
      - subscriber:
          operations:
            - add
              Add a subscriber to a list
            - count
              Count subscribers
            - delete
              Delete a subscriber from a list
            - remove
              Unsubscribe user from a list
            - status
              Get the status of subscriber

  Use get_node_types with discriminators:
    get_node_types({ nodeIds: [{ nodeId: "n8n-nodes-base.sendyTool", resource: "campaign", operation: "create" }] })

- n8n-nodes-base.sms77Tool
  Display Name: seven Tool
  Version: 1
  Description: Send SMS and make text-to-speech calls
  Discriminators:
    resource:
      - sms:
          operations:
            - send
              Send SMS
      - voice:
          operations:
            - send
              Converts text to voice and calls a given number

  Use get_node_types with discriminators:
    get_node_types({ nodeIds: [{ nodeId: "n8n-nodes-base.sms77Tool", resource: "sms", operation: "send" }] })

- n8n-nodes-base.segmentTool
  Display Name: Segment Tool
  Version: 1
  Description: Consume Segment API
  Discriminators:
    resource:
      - group:
          Group lets you associate an identified user with a group
          operations:
            - add
              Add a user to a group
      - identify:
          Identify lets you tie a user to their actions
          operations:
            - create
              Create an identity
      - track:
          Track lets you record events
          operations:
            - event
              Record the actions your users perform. Every action triggers an event, which can also have associated properties.
            - page
              Record page views on your website, along with optional extra information about the page being viewed

  Use get_node_types with discriminators:
    get_node_types({ nodeIds: [{ nodeId: "n8n-nodes-base.segmentTool", resource: "group", operation: "add" }] })
